name: LiMi Model
description: The OME element is a container for all information objects accessible by OME. These information objects include descriptions of the imaging experiments and the people who perform them, descriptions of the microscope, the resulting images and how they were acquired, the analyses performed on those images, and the analysis results themselves. An OME file may contain any or all of this information. With the creation of the Metadata Only Companion OME-XML and Binary Only OME-TIFF files the top level OME node has changed slightly. It can EITHER: Contain all the previously expected elements OR: Contain a single BinaryOnly element that points at its Metadata Only Companion OME-XML file.

children.name: UUID
children.description: This unique identifier is used to keep track of multi part files. It allows the links between files to survive renaming. While OPTIONAL in the general case this is REQUIRED in a MetadataOnly Companion to a collection of BinaryOnly files.

children.name: Creator
children.description: This is the name of the creating application of the OME-XML and preferably its full version. e.g "CompanyName, SoftwareName, V2.6.3456" This is optional but we hope it will be set by applications writing out OME-XML from scratch.

children.name: Rights
children.description: The rights holder of this data and the rights held.

children.name: Rights Holder
children.description: The rights holder for this data. [plain-text multi-line string] e.g..... "Copyright (C) 2002 - 2016 Open Microscopy Environment"

children.name: Rights Held
children.description: The rights held by the rights holder. [plain-text multi-line string] e.g..... "All rights reserved" or "Creative Commons Attribution 3.0 Unported License"

children.name: Project
children.description: The Project ID is required. Datasets can be grouped into projects using a many-to-many relationship. A Dataset may belong to one or more Projects by including one or more ProjectRef elements which refer to Project IDs. Projects do not directly contain images - only by virtue of containing datasets, which themselves contain images.

children.name: Description
children.description: A description for the project. [plain-text multi-line string]

children.name: Experimenter Ref
children.description: This empty element refers to Experimenter that performed this experiment.

children.name: ID
children.description: A unique identifier for this Experimenter.

children.name: Experimenter Group Ref
children.description: This empty element refers to the ExperimenterGroup that owns this Image.

children.name: ID
children.description: A unique identifier for this ExperimenterGroup.

children.name: Dataset Ref
children.description: The DatasetRef element refers to a Dataset by specifying the Dataset ID attribute. One or more DatasetRef elements may be listed within the Image element to specify what Datasetsthe Image belongs to.

children.name: Annotation Ref
children.description: This is an empty element that refers to an Annotation (typically a Comment Annotation that consists of a simple multi-line comment) describing this component.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this element.

children.name: Dataset
children.description: An element specifying a collection of images that are always processed together. Images can belong to more than one Dataset, and a Dataset may contain more than one Image. Images contain one or more DatasetRef elements to specify what datasets they belong to. Once a Dataset has been processed in any way, its collection of images cannot be altered. The ExperimenterRef and ExperimenterGroupRef elements specify the person and group this Dataset belongs to. Projects may contain one or more Datasets, and Datasets may belong to one or more Projects. This relationship is specified by listing DatasetRef elements within the Project element.

children.name: Name
children.description: A name for the dataset that is suitable for presentation to the user.

children.name: Description
children.description: A description for the dataset. [plain-text multi-line string]

children.name: Experimenter Ref
children.description: This empty element refers to Experimenter that performed this experiment.

children.name: ID
children.description: A unique identifier for this Experimenter.

children.name: Experimenter Group Ref
children.description: This empty element refers to the ExperimenterGroup that owns this Image.

children.name: ID
children.description: A unique identifier for this ExperimenterGroup.

children.name: Image Ref
children.description: The ImageRef element is a reference to an Image element.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this component.

children.name: Annotation Ref
children.description: This is an empty element that refers to an Annotation (typically a Comment Annotation that consists of a simple multi-line comment) describing this component.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this element.

children.name: Folder
children.description: An element specifying a possibly heterogeneous collection of data. Folders may contain Folders so that data may be organized within a tree of Folders. Data may be in multiple Folders but a Folder may not be in more than one other Folder.

children.name: Name
children.description: A name for the folder that is suitable for presentation to the user.

children.name: Description
children.description: A description for the folder. [plain-text multi-line string]

children.name: Folder Ref
children.description: The FolderRef element refers to a Folder by specifying the Folder ID attribute. One or more FolderRef elements may be listed within the Folder element to specify what Folders the Folder contains. This tree hierarchy must be acyclic.

children.name: Image Ref
children.description: The ImageRef element is a reference to an Image element.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this component.

children.name: ID
children.description: A unique identifier for this ROI.

children.name: Annotation Ref
children.description: This is an empty element that refers to an Annotation (typically a Comment Annotation that consists of a simple multi-line comment) describing this component.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this element.

children.name: Experiment
children.description: This element describes the experiment during which this Image was acquired. The optional Description element may contain free text to further describe the experiment.

children.name: ID
children.description: A unique identifier for this Experiment.

children.name: Name
children.description: A user assigned name for this Experiment.

children.name: Purpose
children.description: This field records a term used the general purpose of the imaging experiment performed in this case.

children.name: Labelling Method
children.description: This field records a term that describes the method used for the Labelling or Visualization of the structures (i.e. molecular components) under study in this Experiment. The use of a sub-class of the 'Visualization Method' (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/FBbi_00000031) term from the Biological Imaging Methods Ontology (FBbi; https://www.ebi.ac.uk/ols/ontologies/fbbi) is recommended.

children.name: Labelling Method Term Accession
children.description: This field records the Accession ID for the term used to describe the Visualization Method (also often referred to as Labelling Method). The use of a sub-classes of 'Visualization Method' (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/FBbi_00000031) from the Biological Imaging Methods Ontology (FBbi; https://www.ebi.ac.uk/ols/ontologies/fbbi) is recommended.

children.name: Tag
children.description: This field describes small molecules, peptides or full proteins that were used as label in this Experiment. The use of a sub-class of the 'Tag' (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/MI_0507) term from the Molecular Interactions Controlled Vocabulary (MI; http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/mi.owl) is recommended.

children.name: Tag Term Accession
children.description: This records the Accession ID for the term used to describe the Tag used in this Experiment. The use of a sub-class of http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/MI_0507 is recommended.

children.name: Hystochemistry Type
children.description: This field records a term that describes the general category of Histochemistry staining used in this case.

children.name: Experimenter Ref
children.description: This empty element refers to Experimenter that performed this experiment.

children.name: ID
children.description: A unique identifier for this Experimenter.

children.name: Sample
children.description: This field captures a description of the sample that was imaged in this experiment.

children.name: ID
children.description: A unique identifier for this Sample.

children.name: Name
children.description: A user assigned Name for this Sample.

children.name: Biological Origin
children.description: This field captures whether (True) or not (False) this Sample is of Biological Origin.

children.name: Sample Type
children.description: If of Biological Origin, this field records a term that describes the general category of this Sample.

children.name: Live Sample
children.description: This field captures whether (True) or not (False) this Sample was living during Image Acquisition.

children.name: Organism
children.description: If this sample is of biological origin, this field records the biological organisn this sample derives from. A biological is a biological entity that consists of one or more cells and is capable of genomic replication (independently or not). The use terms from NCBITaxon (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCBITaxon_1) is recommended. Note: if the sample is not of biological origin please enter none.

children.name: Organism Term Accession
children.description: This field records the Accession ID for the term used to describe the Organism. The use of the NCBITaxon ontology (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCBITaxon_1) is recommended.

children.name: Organism Part
children.description: If applicable, this field records the part of organism's anatomy or substance arising from an organism from which the biomaterial was derived, excludes cells. E.g. tissue, organ, system, sperm, blood or body location (arm). The use of a sub-class of the 'Organism Part' (http://www.ebi.ac.uk/efo/EFO_0000635) term from the Experimental Factor Ontology (EFO; http://www.ebi.ac.uk/efo/) is recommended.

children.name: Organism Part Term Accession
children.description: This field records the Accession ID for the term used to describe the Organism. A sub-classe of the EFO:0000635 (http://www.ebi.ac.uk/efo/EFO_0000635) term from the Experimental Factor Ontology (EFO; http://www.ebi.ac.uk/efo/) is recommended.

children.name: Sample Preparation Method
children.description: This field records the method that was used to prepare this sample. The use of a sub-class of the 'Sample Preparation Method' (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/FBbi_00000001) term from the Biological Imaging Methods Ontology (FBbi; https://www.ebi.ac.uk/ols/ontologies/fbbi) is recommended.

children.name: Sample Preparation Method Term Accession
children.description: This field records the the Accession ID for the term used to describe method that was used to prepare this sample. A subclass of the Fbbi:00000001 (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/FBbi_00000001) term from the Biological Imaging Methods Ontology (FBbi; https://www.ebi.ac.uk/ols/ontologies/fbbi) is recommended.

children.name: Metrology Sample
children.description: This field captures whether (True) or not (False) this is a Metrology Sample (i.e., a Sample that is used specifcially to calibrate and assess the performance of the Instrument utilized for this experimental Image Acquisition.

children.name: Facing The Objective
children.description: This field captures whether the CoverGlass, the SampleHolder, or neither (None), faced the Objective during Acquisition of this Image.

children.name: Mounting Medium
children.description: This element refers to the Mounting Medium that was used during the acquisition of this Image. According to ISO 10934-1:2002, a Mounting Medium is a liquid, synthetic resin or other medium in which this Specimen is placed during Image Acquisition. NOTE For fixed Samples observed by transmitted-light microscopy, this Medium is transparent, colourless and of specified refractive index, enclosed between the slide and the cover glass. For reflected-light microscopy, the mounting medium is normally a resin with which the Sample is impregnated so that a polished section may be made. For live Samples, the Medium is typically the tissue culture growth substrate.

children.name: Manufacturer
children.description: This field refers to the company, research group or individual who built or manufactured this hardware component.

children.name: Model
children.description: This field refers to the Model name or any other designation the Manufacturer uses for this component.

children.name: Catalog Number
children.description: This field records the Catalog, Part or Serial Number used by the Manufacturer to uniquely identify this component.

children.name: Lot Number
children.description: The Lot Number used by the Manufacturer to refer to a specific production batch or lot for this component.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this component.

children.name: Type
children.description: This field records the general category to which this Mounting Medium belongs.

children.name: Refractive Index
children.description: This field reocrds the nominal Refractive Index of this Mounting Medium, as specified by the Manufacturer.

children.name: Map Annotation
children.description: A Map Annotation. The contents of this is a list of Key/Value pairs.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this element.

children.name: Namespace
children.description: We recommend the inclusion of a namespace for annotations you define. If it is absent then we assume the annotation is to use our (OME's) default interpretation for this type.

children.name: Annotator
children.description: The Annotator is the person who attached this annotation.e.g. If UserA annotates something with TagB, owned by UserB, UserA is still the Annotator.

children.name: Description
children.description: This element contains a description for the annotation.[plain-text multi-line string]

children.name: Annotation Ref
children.description: This is an empty element that refers to an Annotation (typically a Comment Annotation that consists of a simple multi-line comment) describing this component.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this element.

children.name: Value
children.description: This is a Mapping of Key/Value pairs.

children.name: M
children.description: This is a Key/Value pair used to build up a Mapping. The Element and Attribute Name are kept to single letters to minimize the length at the expense of readability as they are likely to occur many times.

children.name: K
children.description: This is the key of this Key/Value pair.

children.name: Specs File
children.description: This element refers to a PDF file that contains the specifications for this hardware component as provided by the Manufacturer.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this element.

children.name: Namespace
children.description: We recommend the inclusion of a namespace for annotations you define. If it is absent then we assume the annotation is to use our (OME's) default interpretation for this type.

children.name: Annotator
children.description: The Annotator is the person who attached this annotation.e.g. If UserA annotates something with TagB, owned by UserB, UserA is still the Annotator.

children.name: Description
children.description: This element contains a description for the annotation.[plain-text multi-line string]

children.name: Annotation Ref
children.description: This is an empty element that refers to an Annotation (typically a Comment Annotation that consists of a simple multi-line comment) describing this component.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this element.

children.name: Binary File
children.description: Describes a binary file.

children.name: Size
children.description: Size of the uncompressed file. [unit:bytes]

children.name: External
children.description: Describes a file location. Can optionally specify a portion of a file using Offset and a ReadLength. If Offset and ReadLength are specified in conjuction with Compression, then they point into the uncompressed file.

children.name: href
children.description: file location

children.name: SHA1
children.description: The digest of the file specified in href.

children.name: Compression
children.description: Specifies the compression scheme used to encode the data.

children.name: Bin Data
children.description: The contents of this element are base64-encoded. These are not CDATA sections, just a base64 stream.

children.name: Compression
children.description: Specifies the compression scheme used to encode the data.

children.name: Big Endian
children.description: This is true if the binary data was written in BigEndian order. This is dependent on the system architecture of the machine that wrote the pixels. True for essentially all modern CPUs other than Intel and Alpha. All Binary data must be written in the same endian order.

children.name: Length
children.description: Character count attribute for the BinData field. This is the length of the base-64 encoded block. It allows easy skipping of the block when parsing the file. [unit:bytes]

children.name: Additional Vendor Information
children.description: This element is intendended to be used as a last-resort solution to capture vendor-specific Additional Information about this hardware component, which cannot be described using either available model elements (Classes or Attributes) or the Map Annotation feature of the schema. The use of this feature should not be intended as a way to hide information that the Imaging Community has designated as essential to ensure rigor and reproducibility. In case this feature is used, the Vendor is fully responsible for this information, including but not limited to, making sure this information is appropriately recorded and kept up to date.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this model element.

children.name: Vendor Name
children.description: The Name of the Vendor responsible for this Information.

children.name: Vendor Bin Data
children.description: The contents of this element are base64-encoded. These are not CDATA sections, just base64 streams.

children.name: Compression

children.name: Length
children.description: Character count attribute for the BinData field. This is the length of the base-64 encoded block. It allows easy skipping of the block when parsing the file. [unit:bytes]

children.description: This is an empty element that refers to an Annotation (typically a Comment Annotation that consists of a simple multi-line comment) describing this component.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this element.

children.name: Cover Glass
children.description: This element describes a rectangular or circular piece of thin glass used to cover a microscopical preparation.

children.name: Manufacturer

children.name: Model

children.name: Catalog Number

children.name: Lot Number

children.name: ID

children.name: Coating
children.description: This field reports a description for any Coating that was layered on the Cover Glass, either by the Manufacturer or after purchase (e.g., Poly-L-Lysine, Collagen).

children.name: Cover Glass No
children.description: This fields reports the numerical designation that describes the expected thickness of this Coverslip (e.g., #1.5) and the relative expected thickness range expressed in mm.

children.name: Thickness
children.description: This fields reports the measured thickness of this Coverslip expressed in mm.

children.name: Sample Holder
children.description: This element describes the device (often referred to as Sample Carrier, Container or Vessel) on which the specimen was mounted for microscopical examination. It typically is flat and it could be made of glass or plastic. Examples include Microscope slides, Petri dishes, and multi-well plates.

children.name: Type
children.description: This field records the Type of this Sample Holder. Options include, microscope slide, Petri dish, tissue culture plate, micro-well plate, etc. (e.g. 35 mm MatTek Glass Bottom Dish).

children.name: Description
children.description: This element holds a free text multi-line optional description for this component.

children.name: Annotation Ref
children.description: This is an empty element that refers to an Annotation (typically a Comment Annotation that consists of a simple multi-line comment) describing this component.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this element.

children.name: Microbeam Manipulation
children.description: Defines a microbeam operation and the region of the image it was applied to. The LightSourceSettingsRef element is a reference to the LightSourceSettings that were used in this case, including a reference to the light source that was used for this micromanipulation experiment (e.g....................... a laser used for photobleaching).

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this MicrobeamManipulation hardware component.

children.name: Type
children.description: This field defines the type of micromanipulation performed.

children.name: Experimenter Ref
children.description: This empty element refers to Experimenter that performed this experiment.

children.name: ID
children.description: A unique identifier for this Experimenter.

children.name: ID
children.description: A unique identifier for this ROI.

children.name: Light Source Settings
children.description: This element holds the Setting applied to a Light Source used in the Light Path during the acquisition of this Channel as well as a reference to the LightSource (the ID attribute) in question.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this component.

children.name: Illumination Power Settings
children.description: This field records the nominal Illumination Power (also known as Power Output or Illumination Intensity) that was set for the Acquisition of this Image or was reported by the Image Acquisition Software, as advertised by the Manufacturer. Depending on the system this might be reported as a % value.

children.name: Attenuation
children.description: This field records the actual Attenuation of the excitation light power that was applied during the Acquisition of this Channel, expressed as a fractional value from 0.0 to 1.0.

children.name: Attenuation Method
children.description: This field records the Method that was used to Attenuate the Laser beam during the acquisition of this Image.

children.name: Used Acousto Optical Device
children.description: In case a Laser was used for this Image Acquisition, this field records whether (True) or not (False) an AcoustoOptical Device was employed for intensity modulation.

children.name: Map Annotation
children.description: A Map Annotation. The contents of this is a list of Key/Value pairs.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this element.

children.name: Namespace
children.description: We recommend the inclusion of a namespace for annotations you define. If it is absent then we assume the annotation is to use our (OME's) default interpretation for this type.

children.name: Annotator
children.description: The Annotator is the person who attached this annotation.e.g. If UserA annotates something with TagB, owned by UserB, UserA is still the Annotator.

children.name: Description
children.description: This element contains a description for the annotation.[plain-text multi-line string]

children.name: Annotation Ref
children.description: This is an empty element that refers to an Annotation (typically a Comment Annotation that consists of a simple multi-line comment) describing this component.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this element.

children.name: Value
children.description: This is a Mapping of Key/Value pairs.

children.name: M
children.description: This is a Key/Value pair used to build up a Mapping. The Element and Attribute Name are kept to single letters to minimize the length at the expense of readability as they are likely to occur many times.

children.name: K
children.description: This is the key of this Key/Value pair.

children.name: Additional Vendor Information
children.description: This element is intendended to be used as a last-resort solution to capture vendor-specific Additional Information about this hardware component, which cannot be described using either available model elements (Classes or Attributes) or the Map Annotation feature of the schema. The use of this feature should not be intended as a way to hide information that the Imaging Community has designated as essential to ensure rigor and reproducibility. In case this feature is used, the Vendor is fully responsible for this information, including but not limited to, making sure this information is appropriately recorded and kept up to date.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this model element.

children.name: Vendor Name
children.description: The Name of the Vendor responsible for this Information.

children.name: Vendor Bin Data
children.description: The contents of this element are base64-encoded. These are not CDATA sections, just base64 streams.

children.name: Compression
children.description: Specifies the compression scheme used to encode the data.

children.name: Big Endian
children.description: This is true if the binary data was written in BigEndian order. This is dependent on the system architecture of the machine that wrote the pixels. True for essentially all modern CPUs other than Intel and Alpha. All Binary data must be written in the same endian order.

children.name: Length
children.description: Character count attribute for the BinData field. This is the length of the base-64 encoded block. It allows easy skipping of the block when parsing the file. [unit:bytes]

children.name: Annotation Ref
children.description: This is an empty element that refers to an Annotation (typically a Comment Annotation that consists of a simple multi-line comment) describing this component.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this element.

children.name: Description
children.description: A description for the Microbeam Manipulation. [plain-text multi-line string]

children.name: Annotation Ref
children.description: This is a simple multi-line comment or annotation describing this component.

children.name: Description
children.description: This element holds a free text multi-line optional description for this component.

children.name: Annotation Ref
children.description: This is an empty element that refers to an Annotation (typically a Comment Annotation that consists of a simple multi-line comment) describing this component.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this element.

children.name: Plate
children.description: This element identifies microtiter plates within a screen. A plate can belong to more than one screen. The Screen(s) that a plate belongs to are specified by the ScreenRef element. The Plate ID and Name attributes are required. The Wells in a plate are numbers from the top-left corner in a grid starting at zero. i.e. The top-left well of a plate is index (0,0).

children.name: Name
children.description: The Name identifies the plate to the user. It is used much like the ID, and so must be unique within the document. If a plate name is not available when one is needed it will be constructed in the following order: 1. If name is available use it. 2. If not use "Start time - End time" (NOTE: Not a subtraction! A string representation of the two times separated by a dash.) 3. If these times are not available use the Plate ID.

children.name: Status
children.description: A textual annotation of the current state of the plate with respect to the experiment work-flow; e.g. 1. Seed cell: done; 2. Transfection: done; 3. Gel doc: todo.

children.name: External Identifier
children.description: The ExternalIdentifier attribute may contain a reference to an external database.

children.name: Column Naming Convention
children.description: The ColumnNamingConvention

children.name: Row Naming Convention
children.description: The RowNamingConvention

children.name: Well Origin X
children.description: This defines the X position to use for the origin of the fields (individual images) taken in a well. It is used with the X in the WellSample to display the fields in the correct position relative to each other. Each Well in the plate has the same well origin. Units are set by WellOriginXUnit. In the OMERO clients by convention we display the WellOrigin in the center of the view.

children.name: Well Origin XUnit
children.description: The units of the well origin in X - default:reference frame.

children.name: Well Origin Y
children.description: This defines the Y position to use for the origin of the fields (individual images) taken in a well. It is used with the Y in the WellSample to display the fields in the correct position relative to each other. Each Well in the plate has the same well origin. Units are set by WellOriginYUnit. In the OMERO clients by convention we display the WellOrigin in the center of the view.

children.name: Well Origin YUnit
children.description: The units of the well origin in Y - default:reference frame.

children.name: Rows
children.description: The number of rows in the plate

children.name: Columns
children.description: The number of columns in the plate

children.name: Field Index
children.description: The index of the WellSample to display as the default Field

children.name: Description
children.description: A description for the plate.

children.name: Well
children.description: A Well is a component of the Well/Plate/Screen construct to describe screening applications. A Well has a number of WellSample elements that link to the Images collected in this well. The ReagentRef links any Reagents that were used in this Well. A well is part of only one Plate. The origin for the row and column identifiers is the top left corner of the plate starting at zero. i.e The top left well of a plate is index (0,0)

children.name: Column
children.description: This is the column index of the well, the origin is the top left corner of the plate with the first column of cells being column zero. i.e top left is (0,0) The combination of Row, Column has to be unique for each well in a plate.

children.name: Row
children.description: This is the row index of the well, the origin is the top left corner of the plate with the first row of wells being row zero. i.e top left is (0,0) The combination of Row, Column has to be unique for each well in a plate.

children.name: External Description
children.description: A description of the externally defined identifier for this plate.

children.name: External Identifier
children.description: The ExternalIdentifier attribute may contain a reference to an external database.

children.name: Type
children.description: A human readable identifier for the screening status. e.g....................... empty, positive control, negative control, control, experimental, etc.

children.name: Color
children.description: A marker color used to highlight the well - encoded as RGBA The default value "-1" is #FFFFFFFF so solid white (it is a signed 32 bit value) NOTE: Prior to the 2012-06 schema the default value was incorrect and produced a transparent red not solid white.

children.name: Well Sample
children.description: WellSample is an individual Image that has been captured within a Well.

children.name: Position X
children.description: The X position of the field (Image) within the well relative to the well origin defined on the Plate. Units are set by PositionXUnit.

children.name: Position XUnit
children.description: The units of the position in X - default:reference frame.

children.name: Position Y
children.description: The Y position of the field (Image) within the well relative to the well origin defined on the Plate. Units are set by PositionYUnit.

children.name: Position YUnit
children.description: The units of the position in Y - default:reference frame.

children.name: Timepoint
children.description: The time-point at which the Image started to be collected

children.name: Index
children.description: This records the order of the well samples. Each index should be unique for a given plate but they do not have to be sequential, there may be gaps if part of the dataset is missing. In the user interface the displayed value of the index will be calculated modulo the number of PlateAcquisitions for the plate.

children.name: Image Ref
children.description: The ImageRef element is a reference to an Image element.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this component.

children.name: Annotation Ref
children.description: This is an empty element that refers to an Annotation (typically a Comment Annotation that consists of a simple multi-line comment) describing this component.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this element.

children.name: Annotation Ref
children.description: This is an empty element that refers to an Annotation (typically a Comment Annotation that consists of a simple multi-line comment) describing this component.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this element.

children.name: Plate Acquisition
children.description: PlateAcquisition is used to describe a single Acquisition run for a plate. This object is used to record the set of images acquired in a single Acquisition run. The Images for this run are linked to PlateAcquisition through WellSample.

children.name: End Time
children.description: Time when the last Image of this Acquisition was collected

children.name: Start Time
children.description: Time when the first Image of this Acquisition was collected

children.name: Maximum Field Count
children.description: The maximum number of fields (well samples) in any well in this PlateAcquisition. This is only used to speed up user interaction by stopping the reading of every well sample.

children.name: Description
children.description: A description for the PlateAcquisition.

children.name: Well Sample Ref
children.description: The WellSampleRef element is a reference to a WellSample element.

children.name: Annotation Ref
children.description: This is an empty element that refers to an Annotation (typically a Comment Annotation that consists of a simple multi-line comment) describing this component.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this element.

children.name: Screen
children.description: The Screen element is a grouping for Plates. The required attribute is the Screen's Name and ID - both must be unique within the document. The Screen element may contain an ExternalRef attribute that refers to an external database. A description of the screen may be specified in the Description element. Screens may contain overlapping sets of Plates i.e. Screens and Plates have a many-to-many relationship. Plates contain one or more ScreenRef elements to specify what screens they belong to.

children.name: Protocol Identifier
children.description: A pointer to an externally defined protocol, usually in a screening database.

children.name: Protocol Description
children.description: A description of the screen protocol; may contain very detailed information to reproduce some of that found in a screening database.

children.name: Reagent Set Description
children.description: A description of the set of reagents; may contain very detailed information to reproduce some of that information found in a screening database.

children.name: Reagent Set Identifier
children.description: A pointer to an externally defined set of reagents, usually in a screeningdatabase/automation database.

children.name: Type
children.description: A human readable identifier for the screen type; e.g. RNAi, cDNA, SiRNA, etc. This string is likely to become an enumeration in future releases.

children.name: Description
children.description: A description for the screen.

children.name: Reagent
children.description: Reagent is used to describe a chemical or some other physical experimental parameter.

children.name: Name
children.description: A short name for the reagent.

children.name: Reagent Identifier
children.description: This is a reference to an external (to OME) representation of the Reagent. It serves as a foreign key into an external database. - It is sometimes referred to as ExternalIdentifier.

children.name: Description
children.description: A long description for the reagent.

children.name: Annotation Ref
children.description: This is an empty element that refers to an Annotation (typically a Comment Annotation that consists of a simple multi-line comment) describing this component.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this element.

children.name: Plate Ref
children.description: The PlateRef element is a reference to a Plate element. Screen elements may have one or more PlateRef elements to define the plates that are part of the screen. Plates may belong to more than one screen.

children.name: Annotation Ref
children.description: This is an empty element that refers to an Annotation (typically a Comment Annotation that consists of a simple multi-line comment) describing this component.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this element.

children.name: Experimenter
children.description: This element describes a person who performed an imaging experiment. This person may also be a user of the OME system, in which case the UserName element contains their login name.Experimenters may belong to one or more groups which are specified using one or more ExperimenterGroupRef elements.

children.name: First Name
children.description: First name, sometime called christian name or given name or forename. [plain text string]

children.name: Middle Name
children.description: Any other names. [plain text string]

children.name: Last Name
children.description: A person's last name sometimes called surname or family name. [plain text string]

children.name: Email
children.description: A person's email address. [valid email address as string]

children.name: Institution
children.description: A person's Institution The organizing structure that people belong to other than groups. A university, or company, etc. We do not specify a department element, and do not mean for Institution to be used in this way. We simply wish to say XXX at YYY. Where YYY has a better chance of being tied to a geographically fixed location and of being more recognizable than a group of experimenters. [plain text string]

children.name: User Name
children.description: This is the username of the experimenter (in a 'unix' or 'database' sense). [plain text string]

children.name: Annotation Ref
children.description: This is an empty element that refers to an Annotation (typically a Comment Annotation that consists of a simple multi-line comment) describing this component.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this element.

children.name: Experimenter Group
children.description: The ExperimenterGroupID is required. Information should ideally be specified for at least one Leader as a contact for the group. The Leaders are themselves Experimenters.

children.name: Description
children.description: A description for the group. [plain-text multi-line string]

children.name: Experimenter Ref
children.description: This empty element refers to Experimenter that performed this experiment.

children.name: ID
children.description: A unique identifier for this Experimenter.

children.name: Leader
children.description: Contact information for a ExperimenterGroup leader specified using a reference to an Experimenter element defined elsewhere in the document.

children.name: Annotation Ref
children.description: This is an empty element that refers to an Annotation (typically a Comment Annotation that consists of a simple multi-line comment) describing this component.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this element.

children.name: Instrument
children.description: This element describes the Instrument used to capture the Image. It is primarily a container referring to the list of hardware components included in a given Microscope. The list consists of the Microscope Stand, Microscope Table, Light Source, Light Source Coupling, Sample Holder, Focusing, Objective, Filter, Dichroic, Additional Lens (including a commonly used Magnification Changer, also known as Optovar), Additional Mirroring Device, Polarization Optics, Prism, Optical Aperture, Optical Device, Detector and Calibration Tool. The Filter element can be either composed of separate Excitation, Emission filters, and Dichroic mirrors or a single Filter Set. An Instrument might have multiple Light Sources, Detectors, Objectives, and Filters, etc. Each of these has its own ID attribute, which can be referred to from the Image, Plane or more commonly Channel elements associated with a given Image Acquisition session. It is understood that the Light Path configuration can be different for each Channel, but cannot be different for each timepoint or each Plane of an XYZ stack.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this component.

children.name: Name
children.description: A User-defined Name for this component.

children.name: Microscope Stand Group
children.description: This element refers to the core structure of this Microscope, which might also be referred to as Body, Frame, or Platform.

children.name: Inverted Microscope Stand
children.description: This is a Microscope in which the object is observed from beneath the stage.

children.name: Upright Microscope Stand
children.description: This is a Microscope in which the object is observed from above the stage.

children.name: Microscope Table
children.description: This element describes the Table used to support this Instrument during Image Acquisition.

children.name: Manufacturer
children.description: This field refers to the company, research group or individual who built or manufactured this hardware component.

children.name: Model
children.description: This field refers to the Model name or any other designation the Manufacturer uses for this component.

children.name: Catalog Number
children.description: This field records the Catalog, Part or Serial Number used by the Manufacturer to uniquely identify this component.

children.name: Lot Number
children.description: The Lot Number used by the Manufacturer to refer to a specific production batch or lot for this component.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this component.

children.name: Name
children.description: A User-defined Name for this component.

children.name: Multiple Microscopes
children.description: This field records whether (True) or not (False) multiple Microscopes share this Microscope Table. This is relevant because multiple Microscopes using the same table might affect stability and vibrations during Image Acquisition.

children.name: Type
children.description: This field describes the type of this Microscope Table.

children.name: Table Width
children.description: The Width of this Microscope Table.

children.name: Table Depth
children.description: The Depth of this Microscope Table.

children.description: A Map Annotation. The contents of this is a list of Key/Value pairs.

children.description: A Unique Identifier for this element.

children.name: Namespace
children.description: We recommend the inclusion of a namespace for annotations you define. If it is absent then we assume the annotation is to use our (OME's) default interpretation for this type.

children.name: Annotator
children.description: The Annotator is the person who attached this annotation.e.g. If UserA annotates something with TagB, owned by UserB, UserA is still the Annotator.

children.name: Description
children.description: This element contains a description for the annotation.[plain-text multi-line string]

children.name: Binary File
children.description: Describes a binary file.

children.name: Size
children.description: Size of the uncompressed file. [unit:bytes]

children.name: External
children.description: Describes a file location. Can optionally specify a portion of a file using Offset and a ReadLength. If Offset and ReadLength are specified in conjuction with Compression, then they point into the uncompressed file.

children.name: href
children.description: file location

children.name: SHA1
children.description: The digest of the file specified in href.

children.name: Compression
children.description: Specifies the compression scheme used to encode the data.

children.name: Bin Data
children.description: The contents of this element are base64-encoded. These are not CDATA sections, just a base64 stream.

children.name: Big Endian
children.description: This is true if the binary data was written in BigEndian order. This is dependent on the system architecture of the machine that wrote the pixels. True for essentially all modern CPUs other than Intel and Alpha. All Binary data must be written in the same endian order.

children.name: Length
children.description: Character count attribute for the BinData field. This is the length of the base-64 encoded block. It allows easy skipping of the block when parsing the file. [unit:bytes]

children.name: Software Group
children.description: This element represents a commercial, or open-source Software, script, macro, plugin or module utilized for any Image Acquisition or Quality Control task related to this Instrument.

children.name: Acquisition Software
children.description: This element represents a commercial, or open-source Software, script, macro, plugin or module utilized for driving this Instrument during Image Acquisition.

children.name: Software Module
children.description: This element represents a commercial, or open-source Software, script, macro, plugin or module utilized for any Image Acquisition, Image Analysis or Quality Control task.

children.name: Calibration Software
children.description: This element describes a software tool utilized to analyze Calibration metrics obtained from this Calibration Procedure and generate a suitable Calibration Reports. As an example, such a Software tool might analyzes microscopy images of fluorescent Colored Beads in order to quantify the Resolution and Chromatic Registration of a fluorescence microsocope over the full Field of View (FOV). An example of such a tool is PSFj (http://www.knoplab.de/psfj/sample-page/). Alternatively, such a Software tool might be used to extract Intensity Calibration results obtained with a specific Intensity Calibration Tool, such as a Power Meter or MetaMax (https://www.umassmed.edu/grunwaldlab/).

children.name: Environmental Control Device
children.description: This element describes an Environmental Control Device available for this Instrument to maintain a controlled Imaging Environment during Imaging Environment.

children.name: Manufacturer
children.description: This field refers to the company, research group or individual who built or manufactured this hardware component.

children.name: Model
children.description: This field refers to the Model name or any other designation the Manufacturer uses for this component.

children.name: Catalog Number
children.description: This field records the Catalog, Part or Serial Number used by the Manufacturer to uniquely identify this component.

children.name: Lot Number

children.name: Name
children.description: A User-defined Name for this component.

children.name: Temperature Control
children.description: This field captures whether this Environmental Control Device provides Temperature control and what type of control it provides. If no Temperature control is provided please enter "None".

children.name: Humidity Control
children.description: This field captures whether this Environmental Control Device provides Humidity control and what type of control it provides. If no Humidity control is provided please enter None.

children.name: CO2Control
children.description: This field captures whether this Environmental Control Device provides CO2 control and what type of control it provides. If no CO2 control is provided please enter None.

children.name: O2Control
children.description: This field captures whether (True) or not (False) this Environmental Control Device provides vibration control.

children.name: Vibration Control
children.description: This field captures whether (True) or not (False) this Environmental Control Device provides O2 control.

children.name: Stand Compatibility
children.description: This field captures what Microscope Stand type this Environmental Control Chamber is compatible with.

children.name: Perfusion
children.description: This field caputers whether (True) or not (False) this Environmental Control Device supports sample Perfusion.

children.name: Lid Material
children.description: In case this Environmental Control Device consists of a Sample Holder Chamber, this field captures the Material that constitutes the Lid of the Chamber.

children.name: Max Temperature
children.description: This field captures the Maximum Temperature that can be stably maintained by this Environmental Control Device.

children.name: Min Temberature
children.description: This field captures the Minimum Temperature that can be stably maintained by this Environmental Control Device.

children.name: Temperature Accuracy
children.description: This field captures the Temperature control Accuracy provided by this Environmental Control Device.

children.name: TTL
children.description: This field captures whether (True) or not (False) this Environmental Control Device can be controlled by a TTL signal.

children.name: Light Source Group
children.description: This element describes one or more Light Source(s) available for this Instrument. An Instrument may have several Light Sources of different types (Laser, Light Emitting Diode, Filament, Arc etc.). A typical fluorescence Microscope has two Light Sources, one for Transmitted light and one for Fluorescence light.

children.name: Arc
children.description: An Arc lamp is a discharge lamp that produces light by an electric arc (also called a voltaic arc), which is typically generated between metal electrodes and travels through an inert gas in a glass bulb. The inert gas can be Mercury (He), Xenon (Xe), or a mixture of the two. The choice of the gas is important because its nature determines the spectral behavior of the lamp. Refer to Type for more informations about this aspect.

children.name: Manufacturer
children.description: This field refers to the company, research group or individual who built or manufactured this hardware component.

children.name: Model
children.description: This field refers to the Model name or any other designation the Manufacturer uses for this component.

children.name: Catalog Number
children.description: This field records the Catalog, Part or Serial Number used by the Manufacturer to uniquely identify this component.

children.name: Lot Number
children.description: The Lot Number used by the Manufacturer to refer to a specific production batch or lot for this component.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this component.

children.name: Name
children.description: A User-defined Name for this component.

children.name: Purchase Date
children.description: This field records the Date in which this Light Source was purchased.

children.name: Illumination Power Reporting Statistic
children.description: This field reports the statistic used by the Manufacturer to report the nominal Illumination Power output emitted by this Light Source at Peak Wavelength.

children.name: Illumination Power Reporting Position
children.description: This field records the Position where the reported Illumination Intensity value was obtained as reported by the Manufacturer.

children.name: Type
children.description: This field records the inert gas vapor contained in the Arc lamp bulb. The nature of the gas determines the spectral behavior of the lamp. At low pressure, Hg lamps emit a characteristic line spectrum, which becomes more uniform as the lamp heats up. On the other hand, Xe lamps emit light of high luminance, high color temperature, and an almost continuous spectrum distributed from the ultraviolet to the infrared.

children.description: This field records the nominal Wavelength associated with the peak response rate of this device.

children.name: Cut On
children.description: CutOn is the Wavelength below which the response rate is below 50%. In case a CutOn Wavelength is not available, such as in the case of a ShortPass or Notch configuration, please enter 000.

children.name: Cut Off
children.description: CutOff is the Wavelength above which the response rate is below 50%. In case a CutOff Wavelength is not available, such as in the case of a LongPass configuration, please enter 000.

children.name: Filament
children.description: A Filament lamp is an incandescent Light Source with a wire filament (usually tungsten), which is heated by the passage of an electric current to such a high temperature that it glows with visible light (incandescence). The emitted spectrum is continuous, and it approximates that of a black-body radiator.

children.name: Type
children.description: This field records the type of Filament lamp Light Source. A Halogen lamp is a Filament lamp whose envelope contains halogen vapor, which reduces the loss of tungsten from the filament. This, in turn, allows to reach a high filament temperature and consequent higher luminance, higher color temperature, and longer operating life than a Standard Filament lamp of the same input power.

children.name: Big Endian
children.description: This is true if the binary data was written in BigEndian order. This is dependent on the system architecture of the machine that wrote the pixels. True for essentially all modern CPUs other than Intel and Alpha. All Binary data must be written in the same endian order.

children.description: Character count attribute for the BinData field. This is the length of the base-64 encoded block. It allows easy skipping of the block when parsing the file. [unit:bytes]

children.name: Illumination Wavelength Range
children.description: This element records a range of Wavelengths at which this Light Source emits, according to the Manufacturer. Multiple of this element may be used.

children.name: Peak Wavelength
children.description: This field records the nominal Wavelength associated with the peak response rate of this device.

children.name: Cut On
children.description: CutOn is the Wavelength below which the response rate is below 50%. In case a CutOn Wavelength is not available, such as in the case of a ShortPass or Notch configuration, please enter 000.

children.name: Cut Off
children.description: CutOff is the Wavelength above which the response rate is below 50%. In case a CutOff Wavelength is not available, such as in the case of a LongPass configuration, please enter 000.

children.name: Illumination Power
children.description: This field records the nominal Illumination Power (also known as Power Output or Illumination Intensity) emitted at Peak Wavelength by this Light Source, as advertised by the Manufacturer.

children.name: Laser
children.description: This element describes a Light Source, which emits coherent radiation at high spectral concentrations of radiance and minimal solid angles. This includes proper Light Amplification by Stimulated Emission of Radiation (LASER) sources that emit light through a process of optical amplification based on the stimulated emission of electromagnetic radiation. It also includes Optical Parametric Oscillator (OPO) sources that use a kind of LASER resonator but based on optical gain from parametric amplification in a nonlinear crystal rather than from stimulated emission.

children.name: Manufacturer
children.description: This field refers to the company, research group or individual who built or manufactured this hardware component.

children.name: Model
children.description: This field refers to the Model name or any other designation the Manufacturer uses for this component.

children.name: Catalog Number
children.description: This field records the Catalog, Part or Serial Number used by the Manufacturer to uniquely identify this component.

children.name: Lot Number
children.description: The Lot Number used by the Manufacturer to refer to a specific production batch or lot for this component.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this component.

children.name: Name
children.description: A User-defined Name for this component.

children.name: Purchase Date
children.description: This field records the Date in which this Light Source was purchased.

children.name: Illumination Power Reporting Statistic
children.description: This field reports the statistic used by the Manufacturer to report the nominal Illumination Power output emitted by this Light Source at Peak Wavelength.

children.name: Illumination Power Reporting Position
children.description: This field records the Position where the reported Illumination Intensity value was obtained as reported by the Manufacturer.

children.name: Type
children.description: This field captures the general category of this Laser. Please note that Optical Parametric Oscillators (OPO) are listed here even though they are technically not a LASER. They produce optical gain not through stimulated emission but via parametric amplification in a non-linear crystal.

children.name: Tuneable
children.description: This field records whether (True) or not (False) this Laser is Tunable to different Wavelengths

children.name: Modulation Mechanism
children.description: This field captures the Modulation Mechanism used for this Laser.

children.name: Pulse
children.description: Whether this Laser emits light in the form of optical Pulses (i.e., light flashes; True) or as a Continuous Wave (CW; False).

children.name: Laser Class
children.description: This describes the safety Class this Laser belongs to. Laser Classes are defined based on their potential for causing injury to humans’ eyes and skin.

children.name: Laser Medium
children.description: This field captures the material out of which this Laser is constructed.

children.name: Is Pumped
children.description: Whether (True) or not (False) this is a pumped Laser.

children.name: Is Pump
children.description: Whether (True) or not (False) this laser serves as a Pump for a downstream laser.

children.name: Position
children.description: This field captures the position of this Laser on a Multi Laser Engine.

children.name: Repetition Rate
children.description: In the case this is a pulsed Laser (Pulse is True), this field (also known as Pulse Frequency) describes the frequency of laser pulses. The default unit is Hz.

children.name: Pulse Duration
children.description: In the case this is a pulsed Laser (Pulse is True), this field describes the duration of each Laser pulse.

children.name: Frequency Multiplication
children.description: This field captures the optical frequency multiplication factor that may be specified for this Laser.

children.name: Used Acousto Optical Device
children.description: This field records whether (True) or not (False) an AcoustoOptical Device was utilized during this Calibration Procedure.

children.name: Map Annotation
children.description: A Map Annotation. The contents of this is a list of Key/Value pairs.

children.description: A Unique Identifier for this element.

children.name: Namespace
children.description: We recommend the inclusion of a namespace for annotations you define. If it is absent then we assume the annotation is to use our (OME's) default interpretation for this type.

children.name: Annotator
children.description: The Annotator is the person who attached this annotation.e.g. If UserA annotates something with TagB, owned by UserB, UserA is still the Annotator.

children.name: Light Emitting Diode
children.description: A Light Emitting Diode (LED) is a semiconductor light source that emits light when current flows through it. Electrons in the semiconductor recombine with electron holes, releasing energy in the form of photons. The color of the light (corresponding to the energy of the photons) is determined by the energy required for electrons to cross the band gap of the semiconductor. White light may be obtained by using multiple semiconductors or a layer of light-emitting phosphor on the semiconductor device.

children.name: Multiport Switch Time

children.name: Light Source Coupling Group
children.description: This element describes the device(s)/method(s) used to couple the available light source(s) with this microscope body in order to transfer light to the specimen. A typical light-microscope has a light coupling device for each available light source.

children.name: Free Beam
children.description: A Free Beam coupling between the Light Source and the Microscope Stand consists of either a direct mount or an optical path that directs light across free space to the back of the Microscope Stand.

children.name: Manufacturer
children.description: This field refers to the company, research group or individual who built or manufactured this hardware component.

children.name: Model
children.description: This field refers to the Model name or any other designation the Manufacturer uses for this component.

children.name: Catalog Number
children.description: This field records the Catalog, Part or Serial Number used by the Manufacturer to uniquely identify this component.

children.name: Lot Number
children.description: The Lot Number used by the Manufacturer to refer to a specific production batch or lot for this component.

children.description: A Unique Identifier for this component.

children.name: Name
children.description: A User-defined Name for this component.

children.name: Attenuation
children.description: This field reports a measure of the capacity of this Light Source Coupling device to attenuate the Illumination Power of the light passing through measured at the Peak Wavelength and expressed in dB/km.

children.name: Transmittance
children.description: The maximum amount of light this Light Source Coupling device is capable of transmitting at the Peak Wavelength. It is represented as a fractional value from 0.0 to 1.0.

children.name: Direct Mount
children.description: Whether light traveling via this Light Source Coupling device from the Light Source to the Microscope Stand travels via a direct mount attached to the Microscope Stand (True), or across an optical path through the air (False).

children.name: Mount Type
children.description: In case the DirectMount field is True, this field records the type of Direct Mount utilized to couple the Light Source to this Microscope Stand.

children.name: Light Guide
children.description: A Light Guide coupling between the Light Source and the Microscope Stand generally consists of a casing enclosing a medium (i.e., typically fiber optic) capable of transferring light from the illuminator to the back of the Microscope Stand.

children.name: Manufacturer
children.description: This field refers to the company, research group or individual who built or manufactured this hardware component.

children.name: Model

children.name: Catalog Number
children.description: This field records the Catalog, Part or Serial Number used by the Manufacturer to uniquely identify this component.

children.name: Attenuation
children.description: This field reports a measure of the capacity of this Light Source Coupling device to attenuate the Illumination Power of the light passing through measured at the Peak Wavelength and expressed in dB/km.

children.name: Transmittance
children.description: The maximum amount of light this Light Source Coupling device is capable of transmitting at the Peak Wavelength. It is represented as a fractional value from 0.0 to 1.0.

children.name: Type
children.description: This field records the general category of Light Guide used in this case.

children.name: Refractive Index
children.description: This field records the Refractive Index of the material used to construct this Light Guide.

children.name: Numerical Aperture
children.description: The Numerical Aperture (NA) of this device expressed as a floating-point (real) number. NA can be calculated from the Angular Aperture (alpha) using the following formula, NA = n(sin alpha), and is a measure of how much of the incident light actually passes through this device. The larger the angular aperture, the more information this device can transmit. Expected range 0.02 - 1.5.

children.name: Acceptance Angle
children.description: The angle within which the light beam produced by a Light Source may be coupled with the bound modes of this Light Guide.

children.name: Geometry
children.description: This field records the cross-section shape of this Light Guide.

children.name: Material Name
children.description: This field records the chemical composition or the non-proprietary trade designation of the material used to construct this Light Guide.

children.name: Single Mode
children.description: This field records whether (True) this Light Guide is single-mode. If not (False), then the guide is assumend to be multi-modal.

children.name: Waveguide Mode
children.description: This field records the transverse mode (or superposition of such modes) that are being imposed by this light guide onto the light wave traversing it. These modes generally follow different propagation constants.

children.name: Diameter
children.description: This fields records the outer diameter of this Light Guide.

children.description: This element records a range of Wavelengths this component is designed for, according to the Manufacturer. Multiple of this element may be used.

children.name: Peak Wavelength
children.description: This field records the nominal Wavelength associated with the peak response rate of this device.

children.name: Cut On
children.description: CutOn is the Wavelength below which the response rate is below 50%. In case a CutOn Wavelength is not available, such as in the case of a ShortPass or Notch configuration, please enter 000.

children.name: Cut Off
children.description: CutOff is the Wavelength above which the response rate is below 50%. In case a CutOff Wavelength is not available, such as in the case of a LongPass configuration, please enter 000.

children.description: This element consists of a file containing the Wavelength profile of this hardware component.

children.name: Objective
children.description: The Microscope's Objective lens consists of a lens, its mount, and any associated parts. It is the part of the imaging system, which forms a primary image of the object, either alone or in conjunction with a tube lens. The Objective typically consists of a compound lens consisting of along a common axis.

children.name: Manufacturer
children.description: This field refers to the company, research group or individual who built or manufactured this hardware component.

children.name: Model
children.description: This field refers to the Model name or any other designation the Manufacturer uses for this component.

children.name: Catalog Number
children.description: This field records the Catalog, Part or Serial Number used by the Manufacturer to uniquely identify this component.

children.name: Lot Number
children.description: The Lot Number used by the Manufacturer to refer to a specific production batch or lot for this component.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this component.

children.name: Name
children.description: A User-defined Name for this component.

children.name: Magnification
children.description: This field captures the Magnification of the Objective as specified by the Manufacturer - i.e., 60 represents a 60X lens.

children.name: Lens NA
children.description: This field captures the nominal Numerical Aperture (NA) of this Objective as specified by the Manufactuer, expressed as a floating-point (real) number (e.g. 1.27 NA). NA is a number that was defined initially by Abbe to characterize Objectives and Condensers. NA can be calculated by the expression n sin alpha, where n is the Refractive Index of the medium between the Lens and the Sample, and alpha is half the Angular Aperture of the Lens. NA is a measure of how much of the illumination that leaves the specimen actually passes through the Lens. The larger the NA, the more information the lens can transmit. Expected range 0.02 - 1.5.

children.name: Correction
children.description: This field captures the type of optical correction (i.e. chromatic correction, or flatness correction, etc.) associated with this Objective.

children.name: Immersion Type
children.description: According to ISO 10934-1:2002, an Immersion Liquid is a liquid (commonly immersion oil, water or glycerol) specified as suitable for use in the space between the front of an immersion lens and the object NOTE Because the immersion liquid is considered in the computing of corrections to be part of the lens, its refractive index and dispersion (or Abbe number) are critical. Please refer to ISO 8036-1 for more details on Immersion Oil. This field describes the general category of the Immersion Liquid this Objective is designed for, according to the Manufacturer. If the general category is not Air, the actual Immersion Liquid that was used during the Acquisition of a specific Image must be specified in Objective Settings.

children.name: Infinity Corrected
children.description: This field records whether (True) or not (False) this Objective is infinity corrected. An Infinity-corrected Objective is corrected for an infinite sample to primary image distance and needs to be used with a Tube Lens of appropriate focal length to obtains its nominal magnification.

children.name: Contrast Modulation
children.description: This field specifies the presence in this Objective of a contrast modulation device, such as a Phase Ring (Ph1, Ph2, Ph3), a Hoffman modulator, a Varel modulator, or similar.

children.name: DIC
children.description: This field specifies whether (True) the Objective is designed for Differential Interference Contrast (DIC) or not (False). DIC is a contrast modality due to double-beam interference in which two waves that fall on the object Plane or Image Plane are separated laterally by a distance similar to the minimum resolvable distance.

children.name: Light Type
children.description: This field specifies whether this Objective is designed for a special Light application (e.g., IR or UV).

children.name: Dipping Medium
children.description: In case the Immersion Type for this Objective is designated as Dipping, this field captures the type of Dipping Medium this Objective is compatible with. Examples include, water-based (biological buffers, culture media, other water-based solutions) organic-based [FocusClear (CLARITY); CUBIC-1, CUBIC-2, CUBIC-R1, CUBIC-R2; 2,2-thiodiethanol (TDE); ethyl cinnamate; Benzyl Benzoate and BB-PEG (PEGASOS); Benzyl Alcohol, Benzyl Benzoate (BABB); dibenzyl ether (DBE); 100% Ethanol].

children.name: Working Distance
children.description: This field reports the Working Distance of this Objective under normal operating conditions. The Working Distance is the distance (in air, or in the specified immersion liquid) between the front of an Objective and the surface of the cover glass or of the specimen if uncovered.

children.name: Correction Collar
children.description: This field records whether (True) or not (False) the Objective was fitted with a Correction Collar. When using high NA dry Objectives (NA 0.8 or greater), cover glass thickness variations of only a few micrometers, immersion medium, and temperature changes result in dramatic image degradation due to aberration. To compensate for these errors, the more highly corrected Objectives are equipped with a Correction Collar mechanism, which is provided to adjust the central lens group position to compensate for deviations from correct optical path length due to changes in Cover Glass Thickness, the nature of the Immersion Liquid, different Numerical Apertures, and Temperature.

children.name: Correction Collar Type
children.description: This field records the type of Correction Collar with which this Objective is fitted. In case the Objective has more than one Correction Collar please enter "Multi" and provide more details in Description.

children.name: Phase Contrast Designation
children.description: If the Objective is designed for Phase, this field specifies the Phase Contrast type designation (see: https://www.microscopyu.com/techniques/phase-contrast/phase-contrast-Microscope-configuration).

children.name: Calibrated Magnification
children.description: This field captures the Magnification of the Objective as measured via a calibration process- i.e., '59.987' for a 60x lens.

children.name: Iris
children.description: This field records whether (True) or not (False) the Objective was fitted with an Iris. Manufacturers offer high NA Objectives, which are equipped with an internal Iris Diaphragm that increases image contrast during digital imaging to compensate for image flare artifacts due to light emitted from areas outside the focal plane. Opening or closing the Iris Diaphragm determines the size of the Objective rear aperture yielding a variable NA range between 0.5 and the Objective's upper limit.

children.name: Objective View Field
children.description: This field records the maximal diameter of the view field allowed by this Objective, expressed in mm, and measured at the intermediate Image Plane. NB: The field of view is influenced by the magnification and diameter of the ocular (eyepiece) field diaphragm, but also by the limits imposed by the design of the Objective.

children.name: Image Distance
children.description: This field records the distance in air between the rear principal Plane of this Objective and the primary Image Plane.

children.name: Front Focal Length
children.description: This field records the distance (f) measured along the optical axis from the front principal Plane of this Lens to the front focal Plane (F).

children.name: Back Focal Length
children.description: This field records the distance (f') measured along the optical axis from the rear principal Plane of this Lens to the rear focal Plane (F').

children.name: Parfocalizing Distance
children.description: This field records the distance between the mounting Plane of this Objective and the specimen Plane.

children.description: The Annotator is the person who attached this annotation.e.g. If UserA annotates something with TagB, owned by UserB, UserA is still the Annotator.

children.name: Optical Assembly Group
children.description: This is a device that might include one or more Lenses, other Optics and Optics Holders. Optical Devices might be custom built or they might also be available as pre-assembled units. Such devices might be embedded within the Microscope Stand or exist as independent units. Examples include the Condenser and the Microscope Tube.

children.name: Beam Collimator
children.description: Beam Collimators are Lens Systems that transform a strongly diverging light beam into a collimated beam, i.e., a beam where light propagates essentially only in one direction, and the beam divergence is weak. Beam Collimators typically contain some kind of Focusing Lens, or sometimes a curved Mirror, and usually some opto-mechanics for stable mounting and possibly adjustment. Beam Expanders can be custom built or they are also available as pre-assembled optical devices.

children.name: Beam Expander
children.description: Beam Expanders are Lens Systems whose role is to increase the diameter of a collimated input beam to a larger collimated output beam. Beam Expanders typically are afocal systems in which object rays enter and exit parallel to the optical axis. As such a Beam Expander does not have a focal length. In most cases, Beam Expanders are realized as optical telescopes consisting of two lenses. Beam Expanders can be custom built or they are also available as pre-assembled optical devices.
