children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this element.

children.name: Annotation Ref
children.description: This is an empty element that refers to an Annotation (typically a Comment Annotation that consists of a simple multi-line comment) describing this component.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this element.

children.name: Coupling Lens
children.description: This element refers to a Lens positioned at the mount connecting the Light Source Coupling device and the Microscope Stand.

children.name: Manufacturer
children.description: This field refers to the company, research group or individual who built or manufactured this hardware component.

children.name: Model
children.description: This field refers to the Model name or any other designation the Manufacturer uses for this component.

children.name: Catalog Number
children.description: This field records the Catalog, Part or Serial Number used by the Manufacturer to uniquely identify this component.

children.name: Lot Number
children.description: The Lot Number used by the Manufacturer to refer to a specific production batch or lot for this component.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this component.

children.name: Name
children.description: A User-defined Name for this component.

children.name: Magnification
children.description: This field records the nominal Magnification provided by this Lens as specified by the Manufacturer.

children.name: Lens NA
children.description: This field captures the Numerical Aperture (NA) of this Lens expressed as a floating-point (real) number. NA is a number that was defined initially by Abbe to characterize Objectives and Condensers. NA can be calculated by the expression n sin alpha, where n is the Refractive Index of the medium between the Lens and the Sample, and alpha is half the Angular Aperture of the Lens. NA is a measure of how much of the illumination that leaves the specimen actually passes through the Lens. The larger the NA, the more information the lens can transmit. Expected range 0.02 - 1.5.

children.name: Infinity
children.description: This field records whether (True) or not (False) this Lens is infinity corrected. An Infinity-corrected Lens is fixed for an infinite sample to primary Image distance. It needs to be used with a Tube Lens of appropriate focal length to obtain the specified magnification.

children.name: Composite
children.description: This field records whether this Lens is Composite (i.e., composed of multiple Lenses; True) or Simple (False).

children.name: Shape
children.description: This field records the shape of the profile (e.g., concave vs. convex) of this Lens. Please note that this attribute is often used to classify Lenses into categories.

children.name: Geometry
children.description: The geometrical shape of this lens' surface(s).

children.name: Diameter
children.description: This field records the physical diameter of this Lens.

children.name: Coating
children.description: An optical Coating consists of one or more thin layers of material deposited on an optical component such as a Lens or Mirror, which alters the way in which the optic reflects and transmits light. This field records the optical Coating used for this Lens.

children.name: Attenuation Coefficient
children.description: This field records the Attenuation Coefficient (AC) of this optical element. The AC (aka blocking coefficient) is a material property that characterizes the degree to which light is being blocked (i.e., scattered or absorbed) by it. AC is used as a measure of the out-of-band blocking of an optical Lens, Filter, Dichroic mirror over an extended range of the spectrum. AC is often reported in units of optical density (OD), whereas OD= -log10(T), and T=Transmittance (0≤T≤1).

children.name: Working Distance
children.description: This field reports the Working Distance of this Lens under normal operating conditions. The Working Distance is the distance (in air, or in the specified immersion liquid) between the front of an Objective and the surface of the cover glass or of the specimen if uncovered.

children.name: Working Distance Unit
children.description: This field reports the Working Distance of this Lens under normal operating conditions. The Working Distance is the distance (in air, or in the specified immersion liquid) between the front of an Objective and the surface of the cover glass or of the specimen if uncovered.

children.name: Image Distance
children.description: This field records the distance in air between the rear principal Plane of this Lens and the primary Image Plane.

children.name: Front Focal Length
children.description: This field records the distance (f) measured along the optical axis from the front principal Plane of this Lens to the front focal Plane (F).

children.name: Back Focal Length
children.description: This field records the distance (f') measured along the optical axis from the rear principal Plane of this Lens to the rear focal Plane (F').

children.name: Radius Of Curvature
children.description: This field records the Radius of Curvature of this Lens. A spherical Lens or Mirror surface has a center of curvature located either along or decentered from the system local optical axis. The vertex of the lens surface is located on the local optical axis. The distance from the vertex to the center of curvature is the Radius of Curvature of the surface.

children.name: Material Name
children.description: This field records the chemical composition or designation of the Material this Lens is made of.

children.name: Glass Code
children.description: If available, this field records the international Glass Code of the material that constitutes this Lens. The Glass Code designation is based on U.S. military standard MIL-G-174, and it consists of a six-digit number specifying the glass according to its Refractive Index nd at the Fraunhofer d- (or D3-) line, and its Abbe Number Vd also taken at that line. The resulting Glass Code is the value of nd-1 rounded to three digits, followed by Vd rounded to three digits, with all decimal points ignored. For example, BK7 has nd = 1.5168 and Vd = 64.17, giving a six-digit Glass Code of 517642.

children.name: Refractive Index
children.description: This field records the Refractive Index of the material this Lens is made of, as specified by the Manufacturer. The Refractive Index is the ratio of the speed of light (more precisely, the phase velocity) in a vacuum versus that in a given medium.

children.name: Abbe Number
children.description: This field records the Abbe number of this Lens. The Abbe Number is a measure of the material's dispersion (variation of Refractive Index versus Wavelength), with high values indicating low dispersion.

children.name: Density
children.description: This field records the Density of the material this Lens is made of.

children.description: A Map Annotation. The contents of this is a list of Key/Value pairs.

children.description: This field records the physical diameter of this Lens.

children.name: Coating
children.description: An optical Coating consists of one or more thin layers of material deposited on an optical component such as a Lens or Mirror, which alters the way in which the optic reflects and transmits light. This field records the optical Coating used for this Lens.

children.name: Attenuation Coefficient
children.description: This field records the Attenuation Coefficient (AC) of this optical element. The AC (aka blocking coefficient) is a material property that characterizes the degree to which light is being blocked (i.e., scattered or absorbed) by it. AC is used as a measure of the out-of-band blocking of an optical Lens, Filter, Dichroic mirror over an extended range of the spectrum. AC is often reported in units of optical density (OD), whereas OD= -log10(T), and T=Transmittance (0≤T≤1).

children.name: Working Distance
children.description: This field reports the Working Distance of this Lens under normal operating conditions. The Working Distance is the distance (in air, or in the specified immersion liquid) between the front of an Objective and the surface of the cover glass or of the specimen if uncovered.

children.name: Working Distance Unit
children.description: This field reports the Working Distance of this Lens under normal operating conditions. The Working Distance is the distance (in air, or in the specified immersion liquid) between the front of an Objective and the surface of the cover glass or of the specimen if uncovered.

children.name: Image Distance
children.description: This field records the distance in air between the rear principal Plane of this Lens and the primary Image Plane.

children.name: Front Focal Length
children.description: This field records the distance (f) measured along the optical axis from the front principal Plane of this Lens to the front focal Plane (F).

children.name: Back Focal Length
children.description: This field records the distance (f') measured along the optical axis from the rear principal Plane of this Lens to the rear focal Plane (F').

children.name: Radius Of Curvature
children.description: This field records the Radius of Curvature of this Lens. A spherical Lens or Mirror surface has a center of curvature located either along or decentered from the system local optical axis. The vertex of the lens surface is located on the local optical axis. The distance from the vertex to the center of curvature is the Radius of Curvature of the surface.

children.name: Material Name
children.description: This field records the chemical composition or designation of the Material this Lens is made of.

children.name: Glass Code
children.description: If available, this field records the international Glass Code of the material that constitutes this Lens. The Glass Code designation is based on U.S. military standard MIL-G-174, and it consists of a six-digit number specifying the glass according to its Refractive Index nd at the Fraunhofer d- (or D3-) line, and its Abbe Number Vd also taken at that line. The resulting Glass Code is the value of nd-1 rounded to three digits, followed by Vd rounded to three digits, with all decimal points ignored. For example, BK7 has nd = 1.5168 and Vd = 64.17, giving a six-digit Glass Code of 517642.

children.name: Refractive Index
children.description: This field records the Refractive Index of the material this Lens is made of, as specified by the Manufacturer. The Refractive Index is the ratio of the speed of light (more precisely, the phase velocity) in a vacuum versus that in a given medium.

children.name: Abbe Number
children.description: This field records the Abbe number of this Lens. The Abbe Number is a measure of the material's dispersion (variation of Refractive Index versus Wavelength), with high values indicating low dispersion.

children.name: Density
children.description: This field records the Density of the material this Lens is made of.

children.description: The Annotator is the person who attached this annotation.e.g. If UserA annotates something with TagB, owned by UserB, UserA is still the Annotator.

children.name: Prism
children.description: This element describes a Prism positioned in the Light Path for one or more Channels available for this Instrument. A Prism is a block of glass or other transparent material with at least two intersecting flat, polished surfaces, used to disperse light or deviate it through an angle. A Prism can be viewed as an ensemble of different rigidly mounted Mirrors, with each face having a permanent orientation with respect to others. A Prism can be employed to deflect or deviate a light beam, rotate or invert an Image, separate polarization states, or disperse light into its component Wavelengths. An Instrument may have several Prisms with different functionality such as Polarizing, Reflecting, or Refracting (or Dispersing). Polarizing Prisms are birefringent crystals that divide incident non-polarized light into separate components polarized orthogonally to each other. Reflecting Prisms are useful for redirecting light beams by total internal reflection. Refracting (or Dispersing) Prisms can be employed to bend and separate light into its component colors. This element can be associated with an optional Optics Turret.

children.name: Manufacturer
children.description: This field refers to the company, research group or individual who built or manufactured this hardware component.

children.name: Model
children.description: This field refers to the Model name or any other designation the Manufacturer uses for this component.

children.name: Catalog Number
children.description: This field records the Catalog, Part or Serial Number used by the Manufacturer to uniquely identify this component.

children.name: Lot Number
children.description: The Lot Number used by the Manufacturer to refer to a specific production batch or lot for this component.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this component.

children.name: Name
children.description: A User-defined Name for this component.

children.name: DIC
children.description: This field records whether this Prism is used for Differential Interference Contrast (DIC) microscopy and, if so, what DIC-compatible type it is (i.e., deSenarmont, Nomarski or Wollaston).

children.name: Function
children.description: This field records the function of this Prism. Beam-shaping (i.e., Anamorphic) Prism pairs are used for reshaping the profile of a laser beam. For example, the elliptical beam from a laser diode can be transformed into a beam with circular cross-section, by magnifying the elliptical beam in one dimension. They can also be used to convert a circular beam into an elliptical beam. Dispersing prisms can be employed to bend and separate light into its component wavelengths. Polarizing prisms (i.e., Glan–Thompson, Nomarski, Wollaston) are birefringent crystals, which divide incident non-polarized light into separate components polarized orthogonally to each other. Reflecting prisms are useful for redirecting (i.e., flip, invert, rotate, etc.) light beams by total internal reflection or for dividing them into two or more separate beams (i.e., beam-splitting) one.

children.name: Condenser
children.description: This field records whether this Prism is within the Condenser (True) or the Objective (False).

children.name: Oil Objective
children.description: This field records whether this Prism is designed to work with oil Objectives (True) or not (False).

children.name: Compound
children.description: This field records whether this Prism is a Compound Prism (i.e., a set of multiple triangular prism elements placed in contact, and often cemented together to form a solid assembly; True) or a Simple Prism (False).

children.name: Refractive Index
children.description: This field records the Refractive Index of the material this Prism is made of, as specified by the Manufacturer.

children.name: Reflectance
children.description: This field records the maximum amount of light this Prism is capable of transmitting at peak Wavelength. It is represented as a fractional value from 0.0 to 1.0.

children.name: Angle Of Incidence
children.description: This field records the Angle Of Incidence (AOI) is the angle between a beam of light incident on the Prism surface and the line perpendicular to the surface at the point of incidence, called the normal.

children.name: Deviation Angle
children.description: This field records the Deviation Angle of this Prism. A beam passing through an object like a prism is deflected twice: once entering, and again when exiting. The sum of these two deflections is called the deviation angle and is measured with respect to the incident ray.

children.name: Prism Angle
children.description: This field records the Angle between two surfaces of this Prism.

children.name: Design
children.description: This field records the shape of the Prism with specific reference to the angles defined by its faces.

children.name: Abbe Number
children.description: This field records the Abbe number of this Prism. The Abbe Number is a measure of the material's dispersion (variation of Refractive Index versus Wavelength), with high values indicating low dispersion.

children.name: Material Name
children.description: This field records the chemical composition or designation of the Material this Prism is made of.

children.name: Glass Code
children.description: If available, this field records the international Glass Code of the material that constitutes this Prism. The Glass Code designation is based on U.S. military standard MIL-G-174, and it consists of a six-digit number specifying the glass according to its Refractive Index nd at the Fraunhofer d- (or D3-) line, and its Abbe Number Vd also taken at that line. The resulting Glass Code is the value of nd-1 rounded to three digits, followed by Vd rounded to three digits, with all decimal points ignored. For example, BK7 has nd = 1.5168 and Vd = 64.17, giving a six-digit Glass Code of 517642.

children.name: Density
children.description: This field records the Density of the material this Prism is made of.

children.name: Cut Off

children.name: Polarization Optics
children.description: This element describes a Polarization device positioned in the Ligth Path for this Instrument. A Polarization device is an optical filter rotatable through 360° that lets light waves of a specific polarization pass through while blocking light waves of other polarizations. This element can be associated with an optional Optics Turret.

children.name: Manufacturer
children.description: This field refers to the company, research group or individual who built or manufactured this hardware component.

children.name: Model
children.description: This field refers to the Model name or any other designation the Manufacturer uses for this component.

children.name: Catalog Number
children.description: This field records the Catalog, Part or Serial Number used by the Manufacturer to uniquely identify this component.

children.name: Lot Number
children.description: The Lot Number used by the Manufacturer to refer to a specific production batch or lot for this component.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this component.

children.name: Name
children.description: A User-defined Name for this component.

children.name: Function
children.description: This field records whether this Polarization Optics is used to generate (Polarizer) or to analyze (Analyzer) polarized light.

children.name: Cross Polarizer
children.description: This field records whether (True) or not (False) this Polarization Optics is a combination of an Analyzer and a Polarizer positioned orthogonal to each other.

children.name: DIC
children.description: This field records whether (True) or not (False) this PolarizationOptics is used for Differential Interference Contrast (DIC) microscopy.

children.name: Beam Splitter
children.description: This field records whether (True) or not (False) this is PolarizationOptics has a beam splitter function.

children.name: Faraday Isolator
children.description: This field records whether (True) or not (False) this PolarizationOptics is a Fraday Isolator. A Faraday Isolator is an optical component which allows light to pass through in only ONE direction and therefore protects laser systems against back reflection. It uses the Faraday magneto-optical effect to turn the polarization of light between various polarizers.

children.name: Construction
children.description: This field records the modality with which this PolarizationOptics is constructed.

children.name: Retardation
children.description: This field records the Phase shift that exists between the polarized light component projected along the fast axis and the part projected along the slow axis.

children.name: Fabrication
children.description: This field records the way in with which this PolarizationOptics is fabricated.

children.name: Material Name
children.description: This field records the chemical composition or designation of the Material this Polarization Optics is made of.

children.name: Glass Code
children.description: If available, this field records the international Glass Code of the material that constitutes this Lens. The Glass Code designation is based on U.S. military standard MIL-G-174, and it consists of a six-digit number specifying the glass according to its Refractive Index nd at the Fraunhofer d- (or D3-) line, and its Abbe Number Vd also taken at that line. The resulting Glass Code is the value of nd-1 rounded to three digits, followed by Vd rounded to three digits, with all decimal points ignored. For example, BK7 has nd = 1.5168 and Vd = 64.17, giving a six-digit Glass Code of 517642.

children.name: Detector Group
children.description: This element describes a Detector available for this Instrument. An Instrument may have more than one Detector of different types, such as a Photomultiplier, or a Camera. The Detector ID can be used as a reference within the Channel element. The values stored in Detector represent the fixed values, variable values modified during the Acquisition go in DetectorSettings.

children.description: This field refers to the company, research group or individual who built or manufactured this hardware component.

children.name: Model
children.description: This field refers to the Model name or any other designation the Manufacturer uses for this component.

children.name: Catalog Number
children.description: This field records the Catalog, Part or Serial Number used by the Manufacturer to uniquely identify this component.

children.name: Lot Number
children.description: The Lot Number used by the Manufacturer to refer to a specific production batch or lot for this component.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this component.

children.name: Name
children.description: A User-defined Name for this component.

children.name: Mount
children.description: This field records the type of Mount used to connect this detector to the Microscope Stand.

children.name: Max Bit Depth
children.description: This field records the Maximum Bit Depth provided by the Detector's Analog to Digital Converter (ADC; also referred to as Digitizer).

children.name: Quantum Efficiency
children.description: This field records the Quantum Efficiency observed at Peak Wavelength, according to the Manufacturer. Quantum Efficiency is generally defined as is the ratio of incident photons to converted electrons. Typically expressed as a number between 0 and 1.

children.name: Electronic Conversion Factor
children.description: This field records the Electron-Conversion Factor for this Detector, as specified by the Manufacturer. The Electron Conversion Factor relates the actual number of electrons generated by the Detector with the digital number associated with each Pixel. It is expressed in electrons/Analog-to-Digital-Unit (ADU). Note that ADU can also be referred to as Digital Number (DN) or Binary Number. It is sometimes referred to as Digital Gain or, in case of CCD cameras as CCD Gain.

children.name: Read Out Noise
children.description: This field captures the Read-out Noise associated with this Detector, as specified by the Manufacturer. Read-out Noise is a constant amount of noise and it includes thermal noise build-up and clock induced charge added by the CCD chip, the readout amplifier and surrounding electronics. It is generally expressed in photo-electrons (i.e., e- root-mean-square).

children.name: Detector Noise Model
children.description: This field records the Noise Model associated with this Detector.

children.name: Dark Current Rate
children.description: This field records the Dark Current Rate of this Detector as specified by the Manufacturer. The Dark Current Rate is the median current detected in the absence of light. Generally expressed in e-/Pixel/sec.

children.name: Dynamic Range
children.description: This field records the Dynamic Range of this Detector in deciBel (dB).

children.name: Ambient Operating Temperature
children.description: This field records the Ambient Temperature at which this Detector is designed to operate, according to the Manufacturer.

children.name: Ambient Operating Humidity
children.description: This field records the Ambient Humidity at which this detector is designed to operate, according to the Manufacturer.

children.name: Map Annotation
children.description: A Map Annotation. The contents of this is a list of Key/Value pairs.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this element.

children.name: Namespace
children.description: We recommend the inclusion of a namespace for annotations you define. If it is absent then we assume the annotation is to use our (OME's) default interpretation for this type.

children.name: Annotator
children.description: The Annotator is the person who attached this annotation.e.g. If UserA annotates something with TagB, owned by UserB, UserA is still the Annotator.

children.name: Description
children.description: This element contains a description for the annotation.[plain-text multi-line string]

children.name: Compression
children.description: Specifies the compression scheme used to encode the data.

children.name: Big Endian
children.description: This is true if the binary data was written in BigEndian order. This is dependent on the system architecture of the machine that wrote the pixels. True for essentially all modern CPUs other than Intel and Alpha. All Binary data must be written in the same endian order.

children.name: Length
children.description: Character count attribute for the BinData field. This is the length of the base-64 encoded block. It allows easy skipping of the block when parsing the file. [unit:bytes]

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this element.

children.name: Wavelength Range
children.description: This element records the Range of Wavelengths this device is designed for, according to the Manufacturer. Multiple of this element may be used.

children.name: Peak Wavelength
children.description: This field records the nominal Wavelength associated with the peak response rate of this device.

children.name: Cut On
children.description: CutOn is the Wavelength below which the response rate is below 50%.

children.name: Cut Off
children.description: CutOff is the Wavelength above which the response rate is below 50%.

children.name: Wavelength Profile
children.description: The location of a file containing the Wavelength profile of this hardware component.

children.name: Map
children.description: This is a Mapping of Key/Value pairs used to store customized metadata that describes this element.

children.name: M
children.description: This is a Key/Value pair used to build up a Mapping. The Element and Attribute Name are kept to single letters to minimize the length at the expense of readability as they are likely to occur many times.

children.name: K
children.description: This is the key of this Key/Value pair.

children.name: Camera Group
children.description: This element describes a Camera available for this Instrument. A Camera is a device that converts optical brightness into an electrical signal using a grid of pixel sensor unit cells and then reproduces an image of the specimen. An Instrument may have more than one camera of different types, such as traditional CCD, EMCCD, Intensified EMCCD, sCMOS, or Analog Video.

children.name: CCD
children.description: This element represents a Charged Coupled Device Camera.

children.name: Dynamic Range
children.description: This field records the Dynamic Range of this Detector in deciBel (dB).

children.name: Ambient Operating Temperature
children.description: This field records the Ambient Temperature at which this Detector is designed to operate, according to the Manufacturer.

children.name: Ambient Operating Humidity
children.description: This field records the Ambient Humidity at which this detector is designed to operate, according to the Manufacturer.

children.name: Array Width
children.description: This field records the Width of this Camera chip or sensor area, expressed in Pixel.

children.name: Array Height
children.description: This field records the Height of this Camera chip or sensor area, expressed in Pixel.

children.name: Pixel Width
children.description: This field records the Physical Width of each Camera Pixel.

children.name: Pixel Height
children.description: This field records the Physical Height of each Camera Pixel.

children.name: Illumination
children.description: This field records the type of illumination the Camera chip receives.

children.name: Manufacturer Offet
children.description: This field reports the Camera Offset when it is pre-set by the Manufacturer. The Offset is an electronic baseline added to the output signal from the sensor to ensure that the displayed signal level is always a positive number of counts.

children.name: Color
children.description: This field records whether this is a Color Camera and what type it is.

children.name: Pixel Well Capacity
children.description: This field records the maximum amount of charge each Pixel Well can hold, measured in electron counts.

children.name: Maximum Frame Rate
children.description: This field records the maximal rate at which this Camera can acquire a single full-frame.

children.name: Maximum Readout Rate
children.description: This field records the maximal Pixel Readout Rate allowed by this Camera.

children.name: Vertical Clock Speed
children.description: This field records the maximal speed at which each vertical column of pixels in the camera chip can be shifted down and read.

children.name: Type
children.description: This field records the Type of CCD camera used in this case.

children.name: CCDArchitecture
children.description: This field captures the Architecture of this CCD Camera and its associated Transfer Mode.

children.name: Register Well Capacity
children.description: This field records the maximum amount of charge each read-out register well can hold, measured in electron counts.

children.description: This element records the Range of Wavelengths this device is designed for, according to the Manufacturer. Multiple of this element may be used.

children.name: Peak Wavelength
children.description: This field records the nominal Wavelength associated with the peak response rate of this device.

children.name: Cut On
children.description: CutOn is the Wavelength below which the response rate is below 50%.

children.name: Cut Off
children.description: CutOff is the Wavelength above which the response rate is below 50%.

children.name: Wavelength Profile
children.description: The location of a file containing the Wavelength profile of this hardware component.

children.name: CMOS
children.description: This field records a complementary metal–oxide–semiconductor camera

children.name: Manufacturer
children.description: This field refers to the company, research group or individual who built or manufactured this hardware component.

children.name: Model
children.description: This field refers to the Model name or any other designation the Manufacturer uses for this component.

children.name: Catalog Number
children.description: This field records the Catalog, Part or Serial Number used by the Manufacturer to uniquely identify this component.

children.name: Lot Number
children.description: The Lot Number used by the Manufacturer to refer to a specific production batch or lot for this component.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this component.

children.name: Name
children.description: A User-defined Name for this component.

children.name: Mount
children.description: This field records the type of Mount used to connect this detector to the Microscope Stand.

children.name: Max Bit Depth
children.description: This field records the Maximum Bit Depth provided by the Detector's Analog to Digital Converter (ADC; also referred to as Digitizer).

children.name: Quantum Efficiency
children.description: This field records the Quantum Efficiency observed at Peak Wavelength, according to the Manufacturer. Quantum Efficiency is generally defined as is the ratio of incident photons to converted electrons. Typically expressed as a number between 0 and 1.

children.name: Electronic Conversion Factor
children.description: This field records the Electron-Conversion Factor for this Detector, as specified by the Manufacturer. The Electron Conversion Factor relates the actual number of electrons generated by the Detector with the digital number associated with each Pixel. It is expressed in electrons/Analog-to-Digital-Unit (ADU). Note that ADU can also be referred to as Digital Number (DN) or Binary Number. It is sometimes referred to as Digital Gain or, in case of CCD cameras as CCD Gain.

children.name: Read Out Noise
children.description: This field captures the Read-out Noise associated with this Detector, as specified by the Manufacturer. Read-out Noise is a constant amount of noise and it includes thermal noise build-up and clock induced charge added by the CCD chip, the readout amplifier and surrounding electronics. It is generally expressed in photo-electrons (i.e., e- root-mean-square).

children.name: Detector Noise Model
children.description: This field records the Noise Model associated with this Detector.

children.name: Dark Current Rate
children.description: This field records the Dark Current Rate of this Detector as specified by the Manufacturer. The Dark Current Rate is the median current detected in the absence of light. Generally expressed in e-/Pixel/sec.

children.name: Dynamic Range
children.description: This field records the Dynamic Range of this Detector in deciBel (dB).

children.name: Ambient Operating Temperature
children.description: This field records the Ambient Temperature at which this Detector is designed to operate, according to the Manufacturer.

children.name: Ambient Operating Humidity
children.description: This field records the Ambient Humidity at which this detector is designed to operate, according to the Manufacturer.

children.name: Array Width
children.description: This field records the Width of this Camera chip or sensor area, expressed in Pixel.

children.name: Array Height
children.description: This field records the Height of this Camera chip or sensor area, expressed in Pixel.

children.name: Pixel Width
children.description: This field records the Physical Width of each Camera Pixel.

children.name: Pixel Height
children.description: This field records the Physical Height of each Camera Pixel.

children.name: Illumination
children.description: This field records the type of illumination the Camera chip receives.

children.name: Manufacturer Offet
children.description: This field reports the Camera Offset when it is pre-set by the Manufacturer. The Offset is an electronic baseline added to the output signal from the sensor to ensure that the displayed signal level is always a positive number of counts.

children.name: Color
children.description: This field records whether this is a Color Camera and what type it is.

children.name: Pixel Well Capacity
children.description: This field records the maximum amount of charge each Pixel Well can hold, measured in electron counts.

children.name: Maximum Frame Rate
children.description: This field records the maximal rate at which this Camera can acquire a single full-frame.

children.name: Maximum Readout Rate
children.description: This field records the maximal Pixel Readout Rate allowed by this Camera.

children.name: Vertical Clock Speed
children.description: This field records the maximal speed at which each vertical column of pixels in the camera chip can be shifted down and read.

children.name: Type
children.description: This field records the Type of CMOS Camera used.

children.name: Map Annotation
children.description: A Map Annotation. The contents of this is a list of Key/Value pairs.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this element.

children.name: Namespace
children.description: We recommend the inclusion of a namespace for annotations you define. If it is absent then we assume the annotation is to use our (OME's) default interpretation for this type.

children.name: Annotator
children.description: The Annotator is the person who attached this annotation.e.g. If UserA annotates something with TagB, owned by UserB, UserA is still the Annotator.

children.name: Description
children.description: This element contains a description for the annotation.[plain-text multi-line string]

children.name: Annotation Ref
children.description: This is an empty element that refers to an Annotation (typically a Comment Annotation that consists of a simple multi-line comment) describing this component.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this element.

children.name: Value
children.description: This is a Mapping of Key/Value pairs.

children.name: SHA1
children.description: The digest of the file specified in href.

children.name: Bin Data
children.description: The contents of this element are base64-encoded. These are not CDATA sections, just a base64 stream.

children.name: Compression
children.description: Specifies the compression scheme used to encode the data.

children.name: Big Endian
children.description: This is true if the binary data was written in BigEndian order. This is dependent on the system architecture of the machine that wrote the pixels. True for essentially all modern CPUs other than Intel and Alpha. All Binary data must be written in the same endian order.

children.name: Length
children.description: Character count attribute for the BinData field. This is the length of the base-64 encoded block. It allows easy skipping of the block when parsing the file. [unit:bytes]

children.description: This is true if the binary data was written in BigEndian order. This is dependent on the system architecture of the machine that wrote the pixels. True for essentially all modern CPUs other than Intel and Alpha. All Binary data must be written in the same endian order.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this element.

children.name: Wavelength Range
children.description: This element records the Range of Wavelengths this device is designed for, according to the Manufacturer. Multiple of this element may be used.

children.name: Peak Wavelength
children.description: This field records the nominal Wavelength associated with the peak response rate of this device.

children.name: Cut On
children.description: CutOn is the Wavelength below which the response rate is below 50%.

children.name: Cut Off
children.description: CutOff is the Wavelength above which the response rate is below 50%.

children.name: Wavelength Profile
children.description: The location of a file containing the Wavelength profile of this hardware component.

children.name: Point Detector Group
children.description: This element represents any Detector that collects light from a restricted point-like area of an Image. These detectors are typically used in Laser Scanning Confocal Microscopy.

children.name: Photo Multiplier Tube
children.description: A Photomultiplier Tube (PMT) is a photoemissive device in which the absorption of a photon results in the emission of an electron and it is used for the detection of very weak signals. These Detectors work by amplifying the electrons generated by a photocathode exposed to a photon flux. The higher the supplied voltage (PMTVoltage setting), the greater the amplification of the current (PMTGain setting) and thus sensitivity, but also the higher the noise in the acquired image. The amplifier offset (PMTOffset setting) defines black level setting of the image. A PMT has no inherent spatial information of the image.

children.name: Manufacturer
children.description: This field refers to the company, research group or individual who built or manufactured this hardware component.

children.name: Model
children.description: This field refers to the Model name or any other designation the Manufacturer uses for this component.

children.name: Catalog Number
children.description: This field records the Catalog, Part or Serial Number used by the Manufacturer to uniquely identify this component.

children.name: Lot Number
children.description: The Lot Number used by the Manufacturer to refer to a specific production batch or lot for this component.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this component.

children.name: Name
children.description: A User-defined Name for this component.

children.name: Mount
children.description: This field records the type of Mount used to connect this detector to the Microscope Stand.

children.name: Max Bit Depth
children.description: This field records the Maximum Bit Depth provided by the Detector's Analog to Digital Converter (ADC; also referred to as Digitizer).

children.name: Quantum Efficiency
children.description: This field records the Quantum Efficiency observed at Peak Wavelength, according to the Manufacturer. Quantum Efficiency is generally defined as is the ratio of incident photons to converted electrons. Typically expressed as a number between 0 and 1.

children.name: Electronic Conversion Factor
children.description: This field records the Electron-Conversion Factor for this Detector, as specified by the Manufacturer. The Electron Conversion Factor relates the actual number of electrons generated by the Detector with the digital number associated with each Pixel. It is expressed in electrons/Analog-to-Digital-Unit (ADU). Note that ADU can also be referred to as Digital Number (DN) or Binary Number. It is sometimes referred to as Digital Gain or, in case of CCD cameras as CCD Gain.

children.name: Read Out Noise
children.description: This field captures the Read-out Noise associated with this Detector, as specified by the Manufacturer. Read-out Noise is a constant amount of noise and it includes thermal noise build-up and clock induced charge added by the CCD chip, the readout amplifier and surrounding electronics. It is generally expressed in photo-electrons (i.e., e- root-mean-square).

children.name: Detector Noise Model
children.description: This field records the Noise Model associated with this Detector.

children.name: Dark Current Rate
children.description: This field records the Dark Current Rate of this Detector as specified by the Manufacturer. The Dark Current Rate is the median current detected in the absence of light. Generally expressed in e-/Pixel/sec.

children.name: Dynamic Range
children.description: This field records the Dynamic Range of this Detector in deciBel (dB).

children.name: Ambient Operating Temperature
children.description: This field records the Ambient Temperature at which this Detector is designed to operate, according to the Manufacturer.

children.name: Ambient Operating Humidity
children.description: This field records the Ambient Humidity at which this detector is designed to operate, according to the Manufacturer.

children.name: Coating
children.description: This field records the Coating Material used to increase the photoelectric properties of the metallic cathode used in this Detector. The coating releases electrons much more readily than the underlying metal, allowing it to detect the low-energy photons in infrared radiation. The lens transmits the radiation from the object being viewed to a layer of coated glass. The photons strike the metal surface and transfer electrons to its rear side. The freed electrons are then collected to produce the final Image.

children.name: Signal Processing
children.description: This field describes the method employed by this device to process the output signal and convert it into a measure of incident light intensity. In Photon Counting mode the system is set in a manner in which each photon hitting the head of the detector gives either no or 1 discrete pulse. This way by counting the pulses one is sure to be counting individual photons. In Analog mode the system is set so the mean value of all signal pulses is used as a measure of intensity.

children.name: Response Time
children.description: This field records the Time required for the Detector to respond to an optical input.

children.name: Dead Time
children.description: This field records the Time required for the Detector to become responsive again after being hit by a photon.

children.name: Radiant Sensitivity
children.description: This field records the Radiant Sensitivity observed with this Detector at the peak Wavelength

children.name: Collection Efficiency
children.description: This field measures the probability that photoelectrons will land on the effective area of the first dynode of the photomultiplier stage. While there might be dispersion at the second and later dynodes, this tends to have less of an effect on the total collection efficiency as the number of emitted electrons increases greatly.

children.name: Multianode
children.description: This field records whether (True) or not (False) this is Photo Multiplier Tube is composed of an array of multiple

children.name: Multianode Channel Nr
children.description: In case this Detector is a Multianode composed of an array of multiple Photomultiplier Tubes, this field records the number of Channels it consists of.

children.name: Multianode Arrangement
children.description: In case this Detector is a Multianode composed of an array of multiple Photomultiplier Tubes, this field records the arrangement of the individual Photomultiplier Tubes.

children.name: Head On
children.description: This is True if the Photocathode is positioned at either end of the vacuum tube. This is False if the photocathode element is located on the side of the vacuum tube (side-on geometry).

children.name: Map Annotation
children.description: A Map Annotation. The contents of this is a list of Key/Value pairs.

children.description: A Unique Identifier for this element.

children.name: Namespace
children.description: We recommend the inclusion of a namespace for annotations you define. If it is absent then we assume the annotation is to use our (OME's) default interpretation for this type.

children.name: Annotator
children.description: The Annotator is the person who attached this annotation.e.g. If UserA annotates something with TagB, owned by UserB, UserA is still the Annotator.

children.name: Description
children.description: This element contains a description for the annotation.[plain-text multi-line string]

children.name: Wavelength Range
children.description: This element records the Range of Wavelengths this device is designed for, according to the Manufacturer. Multiple of this element may be used.

children.name: Peak Wavelength
children.description: This field records the nominal Wavelength associated with the peak response rate of this device.

children.name: Cut On
children.description: CutOn is the Wavelength below which the response rate is below 50%.

children.name: Cut Off
children.description: CutOff is the Wavelength above which the response rate is below 50%.

children.name: Wavelength Profile
children.description: The location of a file containing the Wavelength profile of this hardware component.

children.name: TIRFHardware Module
children.description: Total Internal Reflection Fluorescence (TIRF) microscopy is a modality in which fluorescence is excited in a thin layer by an evanescent wave produced by total internal reflection. This element describes a Hardware Module that is used to achieve TIRF microscopy.

children.name: Manufacturer
children.description: This field refers to the company, research group or individual who built or manufactured this hardware component.

children.name: Model
children.description: This field refers to the Model name or any other designation the Manufacturer uses for this component.

children.name: Catalog Number
children.description: This field records the Catalog, Part or Serial Number used by the Manufacturer to uniquely identify this component.

children.name: Lot Number
children.description: The Lot Number used by the Manufacturer to refer to a specific production batch or lot for this component.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this component.

children.name: Method
children.description: This field captures the approach used by this TIRF Hardware Module to produce TIRF illumination. In Objective-based TIRF, the excitation lightpath and the emission channel share the same optics, which provides high signals but might result in high background. In Prism-based TIRF excitation light enters the dovetail prism, reflects at the glass/water interface and escapes through the opposite facet without interferring with the emission channel . In Lightguide-based TIRF, excitation light enters the lightguide, reflects multiple times from its top and bottom surfaces to generate wide-area evanescent waves and escapes from the opposite side of the lightguide without interferring with the emission channel.

children.name: RIng
children.description: This field captures whether this TIRF Hardware Module employs Ring (also known as spinning-spot) TIRF (True) or Spot TIRF (False).

children.name: Motorized
children.description: This field captures whether this TIRF Hardware Module employs a Motorized device to set the TIRF Angle (True) or not (False).

children.name: Confocal Scanner Group
children.description: According to ISO-10934-2-2007, a confocal microscope is a microscope in which, ideally, a point in the object plane is illuminated by a diffraction-limited spot of light, and light emanating from this point is focused upon and detected from an area smaller than the central area of the diffraction disc. The confocal principle leads to improved axial resolution by suppression of light from out-of-focus planes. In order to utilize this modality, a Confocal Scanner is used to form an Image of an extended Specimen region by controlling the direction of an outgoing Laser beam, the position of the Specimen or the location of the illuminated and detected spots simultaneously. Refer to https://www.iso.org/standard/69820.html for further details and measurement methods.

children.name: Raster Scanner
children.description: A Raster Scanner is typically used for systematic periodic scanning (raster scanning) of the Laser beam or of the Specimen position, either in one or in two dimensions. Refer to https://www.iso.org/standard/69820.html for further details and measurement methods.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this component.

children.name: Name
children.description: A User-defined Name for this component.

children.name: Nr Of Scan Points
children.description: This field records the number of scanning points available for this scan head.

children.name: Max Frame Rate
children.description: This field captures the Maximum full Frame Rate available for this device, as specificed by the Manufacturer.

children.name: Points Configuration
children.description: This field records a description of the arrangement of scan points (can be spiral, linear, grid, other)

children.name: Type
children.description: This field records the type of this Raster Scanner. In a Galvanometer-mirror based system, two galvanometer scan mirrors (one for each axis) direct the laser beam to any XY position in the field of view. In a Resonant system laser positioning in the x-axis is provided by a special resonant scan mirror which oscillates at a fixed frequency.

children.name: Horizontal Scanning Speed
children.description: This field records the maximal Horizontal Scanning Speed of this device.

children.name: Vertical Scanning Speed
children.description: This field records the maximal Vertical Scanning Speed of this device.

children.name: Annotation Ref
children.description: This is an empty element that refers to an Annotation (typically a Comment Annotation that consists of a simple multi-line comment) describing this component.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this element.

children.name: Spinning Disk
children.description: A Spinning disk confocal scanner is a device in which the scanning of the illuminated and detected spots is performed simultaneously using an opaque disk called a Nipkow disk, which has many ideally identical small holes arranged in Archimedean spirals. Refer to https://www.iso.org/standard/69820.html for further details and measurement methods.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this component.

children.name: Name

children.name: Single Disk
children.description: This records whether this Spinning Disk Confocal Scanner uses Single-Disk (True) or Dual-Disk (False) technology.

children.name: Disk Speed
children.description: This field records the maximal Disk rotation Speed of this device, according to the Manufactuer.

children.name: Annotation Ref
children.description: This is an empty element that refers to an Annotation (typically a Comment Annotation that consists of a simple multi-line comment) describing this component.

children.name: ID

children.name: Acousto Optical Device Group
children.description: This is a device that performs spatial, temporal, and spectral modulations of light on the basis of acousto-optic (AO) diffraction. AO diffraction is a phenomenon whereby an acoustic wave propagating in an optically transparent medium, produces a periodic modulation of the index of refraction of the medium via the elasto-optical effect and might therefore diffract portions of an incident light into one or more directions.

children.name: Acousto Optical Beam Splitter
children.description: An AOBS is actually an AOTF used in an imaginative manner by Leica to replace the dichroic mirror usually used to separate the illumination and the detection paths in a confocal fluorescence Microscope. In practice the AOBS is programmed so that it leaves most of the incoming beam of light undeflected, and it only deflects light originating from specific laser lines. The AOBS allows the simultaneous deflection of multiple laser lines simultaneously towards the sample, while at the same time controlling the intensity of each line independently.

children.name: Manufacturer
children.description: This field refers to the company, research group or individual who built or manufactured this hardware component.

children.name: Model
children.description: This field refers to the Model name or any other designation the Manufacturer uses for this component.

children.name: Catalog Number
children.description: This field records the Catalog, Part or Serial Number used by the Manufacturer to uniquely identify this component.

children.name: Lot Number
children.description: The Lot Number used by the Manufacturer to refer to a specific production batch or lot for this component.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this component.

children.name: Name
children.description: A User-defined Name for this component.

children.name: Contrast Ratio
children.description: This field records the reports on the ratio of maximum versus minimum transmittance.

children.name: Coating
children.description: An optical Coating consists of one or more thin layers of material deposited on an optical component such as a Lens or Mirror, which alters the way in which the optic reflects and transmits light. This field records the thin-film material used to coat the non linear crystal and reduce surface reflection.

children.name: Attenuation Method
children.description: This field records the mean by which this optical element achieves out-of-band transmission attenuation.

children.name: Attenuation Coefficient
children.description: This field records the Attenuation Coefficient (AC) of this optical element. The AC (aka blocking coefficient) is a material property that characterizes the degree to which light is being blocked (i.e., scattered or absorbed) by it. AC is used as a measure of the out-of-band blocking of an optical Lens, Filter, Dichroic mirror over an extended range of the spectrum. AC is often reported in units of optical density (OD), whereas OD= -log10(T), and T=Transmittance (0≤T≤1).

children.name: Tuning Accuracy
children.description: This field measures the accuracy with which the wavelength discriminating capacity of this device can be tuned or changed.

children.name: Polarization
children.description: This field records the polarization effect of this optical element. Dichroic beamsplitters (and, in fact, any thin-film interference coating that is used at non-normal angles-of-incidence) will cause some amount of polarization, the precise effect varying greatly with wavelength and with the particular coating design.

children.name: Bragg
children.description: This indicates whether this device is operated under Bragg regime (True) or not. In Bragg regime, at one particular incidence angle , only one diffraction order is produced - the others are annihilated by destructive interference.

children.name: Tuning Resolution
children.description: This field measures the wavelength discriminating capacity of this device and it is defined as the full width at half-maximum (FWHM) of the main lobe from the selected optical output, and is a function of both wavelength and device configuration.

children.name: Tuning Rate
children.description: This field records the time it takes for this device to be tuned without degrading resolution. This time is determined by the velocity of the acoustic wage as it traverses the aperture of this device.

children.name: Angle Of Incidence
children.description: This field records the optimal angle of incidence (AOI) for this mirror. The AOI is the angle between the optical axis of the incident light and the axis normal to the surface of the mirror. In specular reflection AOI is by definition equal to the Angle Of Reflection (AOR).

children.name: Angular Aperture
children.description: This field records the angular aperture is the half-angle of the cone of light entering the this device. It is, therefore, a measure of how much of the incident light actually passes through the device.

children.name: Material
children.description: This field describes the transparent crystal or glass material used to build this device, through which the light propagates and whose refractive properties can be modulated by acoustic waves of different frequencies.

children.name: Design
children.description: This field describes wheher the acoustic wave is absorbed or reflected by the distal surface of the crystal.

children.name: Geometry
children.description: This field describes the the direction of the applied electric field with respect to the light beam. Longitudinal devices have the electric field in the direction of the light beam, which passes through holes in the electrodes. Transverse devices have the electric field perpendicular to the light beam.

children.name: Operating Temperature
children.description: This field records the ambient temperature at which this device is designed to operate, according to the Manufacturer.

children.name: Acousto Optical Deflector
children.description: Acousto-optic deflectors are devices which can be used to deflect a laser beam in one direction by a variable angle, controlled by the frequency of an electrical signal. Essentially, such a device is an acousto-optic modulator which is operated with an electrical drive signal of constant power but a variable frequency. The direction of the diffracted beam is determined by a Bragg condition, which contains the sound wavelength and thus depends on the sound frequency.

children.description: This field records the optimal angle of incidence (AOI) for this mirror. The AOI is the angle between the optical axis of the incident light and the axis normal to the surface of the mirror. In specular reflection AOI is by definition equal to the Angle Of Reflection (AOR).

children.name: Angular Aperture
children.description: This field records the angular aperture is the half-angle of the cone of light entering the this device. It is, therefore, a measure of how much of the incident light actually passes through the device.

children.name: Material
children.description: This field describes the transparent crystal or glass material used to build this device, through which the light propagates and whose refractive properties can be modulated by acoustic waves of different frequencies.

children.name: Design
children.description: This field describes wheher the acoustic wave is absorbed or reflected by the distal surface of the crystal.

children.name: Geometry
children.description: This field describes the the direction of the applied electric field with respect to the light beam. Longitudinal devices have the electric field in the direction of the light beam, which passes through holes in the electrodes. Transverse devices have the electric field perpendicular to the light beam.

children.name: Operating Temperature
children.description: This field records the ambient temperature at which this device is designed to operate, according to the Manufacturer.

children.name: Acousto Optical Modulator
children.description: An acousto-optic modulator (AOM) is an electronically-tunable device which can be used for controlling the direction and/or intensity of a laser beam by an acoustically-induced diffraction grating in a crystal. It is based on the acousto-optic effect, i.e. the modification of the refractive index of some crystal or glass material by the oscillating mechanical strain of a sound wave (photoelastic effect).

children.name: Manufacturer
children.description: This field refers to the company, research group or individual who built or manufactured this hardware component.

children.name: Model
children.description: This field refers to the Model name or any other designation the Manufacturer uses for this component.

children.name: Catalog Number
children.description: This field records the Catalog, Part or Serial Number used by the Manufacturer to uniquely identify this component.

children.name: Lot Number
children.description: The Lot Number used by the Manufacturer to refer to a specific production batch or lot for this component.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this component.

children.name: Name
children.description: A User-defined Name for this component.

children.name: Contrast Ratio
children.description: This field records the reports on the ratio of maximum versus minimum transmittance.

children.name: Coating
children.description: An optical Coating consists of one or more thin layers of material deposited on an optical component such as a Lens or Mirror, which alters the way in which the optic reflects and transmits light. This field records the thin-film material used to coat the non linear crystal and reduce surface reflection.

children.name: Attenuation Method
children.description: This field records the mean by which this optical element achieves out-of-band transmission attenuation.

children.name: Attenuation Coefficient
children.description: This field records the Attenuation Coefficient (AC) of this optical element. The AC (aka blocking coefficient) is a material property that characterizes the degree to which light is being blocked (i.e., scattered or absorbed) by it. AC is used as a measure of the out-of-band blocking of an optical Lens, Filter, Dichroic mirror over an extended range of the spectrum. AC is often reported in units of optical density (OD), whereas OD= -log10(T), and T=Transmittance (0≤T≤1).

children.name: Tuning Accuracy
children.description: This field measures the accuracy with which the wavelength discriminating capacity of this device can be tuned or changed.

children.name: Polarization
children.description: This field records the polarization effect of this optical element. Dichroic beamsplitters (and, in fact, any thin-film interference coating that is used at non-normal angles-of-incidence) will cause some amount of polarization, the precise effect varying greatly with wavelength and with the particular coating design.

children.name: Bragg
children.description: This indicates whether this device is operated under Bragg regime (True) or not. In Bragg regime, at one particular incidence angle , only one diffraction order is produced - the others are annihilated by destructive interference.

children.name: Tuning Resolution
children.description: This field measures the wavelength discriminating capacity of this device and it is defined as the full width at half-maximum (FWHM) of the main lobe from the selected optical output, and is a function of both wavelength and device configuration.

children.name: Tuning Rate
children.description: This field records the time it takes for this device to be tuned without degrading resolution. This time is determined by the velocity of the acoustic wage as it traverses the aperture of this device.

children.name: Angle Of Incidence
children.description: This field records the optimal angle of incidence (AOI) for this mirror. The AOI is the angle between the optical axis of the incident light and the axis normal to the surface of the mirror. In specular reflection AOI is by definition equal to the Angle Of Reflection (AOR).

children.description: This field records the angular aperture is the half-angle of the cone of light entering the this device. It is, therefore, a measure of how much of the incident light actually passes through the device.

Longitudinal devices have the electric field in the direction of the light beam, which passes through holes in the electrodes. Transverse devices have the electric field perpendicular to the light beam.

children.description: This field records the ambient temperature at which this device is designed to operate, according to the Manufacturer.

children.description: This field describes the category of this device.

children.name: Acousto Optical Tuneable Filter
children.description: This is kind tuneable filter, whose optical filtering properties can be tuned by virtue of acousto-optical deflection. As such, different wavelengths exits the crystal at different angles depending on the applied radio wave frequency. In an optical system in which the input light beam strikes the crystal at a specific angle and can only re-enter the system at a specific angle, then one can choose the wavelength of the light diffracted at the output angle angle by varying the frequency of the acoustic wave.

children.name: Electro Optical Device Group

children.description: A Deformable Mirror (DM) is a Mechano Electro-Optical Device that has a controllable spatially varying mirror surface to modulate the wavefront of a beam of light impinging on it. This type of device is typically used in adaptive optics.

children.name: Electro Optical Modulator
children.description: An Electro-Optical Modulator (EOM) is a device which can be used for controlling the power (→ intensity modulators), phase (→ phase modulators) or polarization of light with an electrical control signal.

children.name: Manufacturer
children.description: This field refers to the company, research group or individual who built or manufactured this hardware component.

children.name: Model
children.description: This field refers to the Model name or any other designation the Manufacturer uses for this component.

children.name: Catalog Number
children.description: This field records the Catalog, Part or Serial Number used by the Manufacturer to uniquely identify this component.

children.name: Lot Number
children.description: The Lot Number used by the Manufacturer to refer to a specific production batch or lot for this component.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this component.

children.name: Name
children.description: A User-defined Name for this component.

children.name: Modulation Mode
children.description: This field records the describes the type of modulation this devices produces on a beam of light.

children.name: Contrast Ratio
children.description: This field records the reports on the ratio of maximum versus minimum transmittance.

children.name: Coating
children.description: An optical Coating consists of one or more thin layers of material deposited on an optical component such as a Lens or Mirror, which alters the way in which the optic reflects and transmits light. This field records the thin-film material used to coat the non linear crystal and reduce surface reflection.

children.name: Attenuation Method
children.description: This field records the mean by which this optical element achieves out-of-band transmission attenuation.

children.name: Attenuation Coefficient
children.description: This field records the Attenuation Coefficient (AC) of this optical element. The AC (aka blocking coefficient) is a material property that characterizes the degree to which light is being blocked (i.e., scattered or absorbed) by it. AC is used as a measure of the out-of-band blocking of an optical Lens, Filter, Dichroic mirror over an extended range of the spectrum. AC is often reported in units of optical density (OD), whereas OD= -log10(T), and T=Transmittance (0≤T≤1).

children.name: Tuning Accuracy
children.description: This field measures the accuracy with which the wavelength discriminating capacity of this device can be tuned or changed.

children.name: Tuning Resolution
children.description: This field measures the wavelength discriminating capacity of this device and it is defined as the full width at half-maximum (FWHM) of the main lobe from the selected optical output, and is a function of both wavelength and device configuration.

children.name: Tuning Rate
children.description: This field records the time it takes for this device to be tuned without degrading resolution. This time is determined by the velocity of the acoustic wage as it traverses the aperture of this device.

children.name: Angle Of Incidence
children.description: This field records the optimal angle of incidence (AOI) for this mirror. The AOI is the angle between the optical axis of the incident light and the axis normal to the surface of the mirror. In specular reflection AOI is by definition equal to the Angle Of Reflection (AOR).

children.name: Angular Aperture
children.description: This field records the angular aperture is the half-angle of the cone of light entering the this device. It is, therefore, a measure of how much of the incident light actually passes through the device.

children.name: Operating Temperature
children.description: This field records the ambient temperature at which this device is designed to operate, according to the Manufacturer.

children.name: Operating Humidity
children.description: This field records the ambient humidity at which this device is designed to operate, according to the manufactuer.

children.name: Warm Up Time
children.description: This field records the time it takes for this device to reach operating conditions according to the Manufacturer.

children.name: Pockels Cell
children.description: Pockels cells are the basic components of electro-optic modulators. A Pockels cell is a device consisting of an electro-optic crystal (with some electrodes attached to it) through which a light beam can propagate. The phase delay in the crystal (→ Pockels effect) can be modulated by applying a variable electric voltage. The Pockels cell thus acts as a voltage-controlled waveplate.

children.name: Half Wave Voltage
children.description: This field record the Voltage required for inducing a phase change equivalent to a half an optical wavelength. In an amplitude modulator, the applied voltage has to be changed by this value in order to go from the operation point with minimum transmission to that with maximum transmission. The HalfWaveVoltage can be referred to as, Uπ, Uλ/2 or Vλ/2.

children.name: Material
children.description: This field describes the nonlinear crystal materials used to contruct this Pockels cell.

children.name: Liquid Crystal Tuneable Filter
children.description: This element describes a liquid crystal tunable filter (LCTF) is an optical filter that uses electronically controlled liquid crystal (LC) elements to transmit a selectable wavelength of light and exclude others.

children.name: Microlens Array
children.description: Micro-lens arrays contain multiple micro-lenses forming a one- or two-dimensional array on a supporting substrate.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this component.

children.name: Name
children.description: A User-defined Name for this component.

children.name: Lenslet Diameter
children.description: This field records the physical Dameter of the Lenslets that compose this Micro Lens Array.

children.name: Lenslet Pitch
children.description: This field records the distance between Lenslets in this Micro Lens Array.

children.name: Lenslet Arrangement
children.description: This field records the arrangement of individual Lenslets on this Array.

children.name: Lenslet Shape
children.description: This field records the geometrical shape (e.g., round vs. square) of the individual Lenslets composing this Micro Lens Array.

children.name: Lenslet Profile Shape
children.description: This field describes the shape of the profile (e.g., concave vs. convex) of the individual Lenslets that compose this Micro Lens Array.

children.name: Coating
children.description: An optical Coating consists of one or more thin layers of material deposited on an optical component such as a Lens or Mirror, which alters the way in which the optic reflects and transmits light. This field records the thin-film material used to coat the non linear crystal and reduce surface reflection.

children.name: Reflectance
children.description: This field records the maximum amount of light this Micro Lens Array is capable of reflecting. It is represented as a fractional value from 0.0 to 1.0.

children.name: Sustrate Material
children.description: This field records the chemical composition or designation of the substrate of this Micro Lens Array. The substrate is typically composed of a glass material with low autofluorescence, such as UV-grade fused silica.

children.name: Front Focal Length
children.description: This field records the distance (f) measured along the optical axis from the front principal plane of this Micro Lens Array to the front focal plane (F).

children.name: Back Focal Length
children.description: This field records the distance (f') measured along the optical axis from the rear principal plane of this Micro Lens Array to the rear focal plane (F').

children.name: Optical Distance Unit
children.description: Use this unit for both Focal Length fields.

children.name: Annotation Ref
children.description: This is an empty element that refers to an Annotation (typically a Comment Annotation that consists of a simple multi-line comment) describing this component.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this element.

children.name: Wavelength Range
children.description: This element records a range of Wavelengths this component is designed for, according to the Manufacturer. Multiple of this element may be used.

children.name: Peak Wavelength
children.description: This field records the nominal Wavelength associated with the peak response rate of this device.

children.name: Cut On
children.description: CutOn is the Wavelength below which the response rate is below 50%. In case a CutOn Wavelength is not available, such as in the case of a ShortPass or Notch configuration, please enter 000.

children.name: Cut Off
children.description: CutOff is the Wavelength above which the response rate is below 50%. In case a CutOff Wavelength is not available, such as in the case of a LongPass configuration, please enter 000.

children.name: Wavelength Profile File
children.description: This element consists of a file containing the Wavelength profile of this hardware component.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this element.

children.description: We recommend the inclusion of a namespace for annotations you define. If it is absent then we assume the annotation is to use our (OME's) default interpretation for this type.

children.name: Annotator
children.description: The Annotator is the person who attached this annotation.e.g. If UserA annotates something with TagB, owned by UserB, UserA is still the Annotator.

children.name: Intensity Calibration Tool
children.description: A tool, such as the Meta-Max calibration toold developed by the 4D Nucleome Imaging Standards Working Group, used to perform an intensity calibration procedure.

children.description: This field refers to the company, research group or individual who built or manufactured this hardware component.

children.name: Model
children.description: This field refers to the Model name or any other designation the Manufacturer uses for this component.

children.name: Catalog Number
children.description: This field records the Catalog, Part or Serial Number used by the Manufacturer to uniquely identify this component.

children.name: Lot Number
children.description: The Lot Number used by the Manufacturer to refer to a specific production batch or lot for this component.

children.name: Type

children.name: Calibration Type
children.description: The type of calibration proceudure this tool is intended to perform.

children.name: Last Calibration Date
children.description: This field records the Date in which the most recent Calibration Procedure of this Calibration Tool was performed.
