children.name: Confocal Scanner Settings
children.description: This element holds the Setting applied to a Confocal Scanner used during the Acquisiton of this Channel as well as a reference (the ID attribute) to the Confocal Scanner in question.

children.name: ID
children.description: This is the Unique Identifier of the device used in this case.

children.name: Preset Pinhole Size
children.description: In case this Confocal Scanner (i.e., Spinning disk, Swept-field, or other Multi-point scanners) has several fixed Pinhole Sizes to choose from, this field records the Pinhole Size that was set during the Acquisition of this Channel.

children.name: Pinhole Spacing
children.description: If applicable, this field records the Spacing between individual Pinholes that was set during the Acquisition of this Channel.

children.name: Pinhole Spacing Unit
children.description: If applicable, this field records the Spacing between individual Pinholes that was set during the Acquisition of this Channel.

children.name: Scan Directionality
children.description: This field records the ScanDirectionality that was set for this device during Acquisition. In Unidirectional scan mode, data are only acquired while the laser beam moves in one direction. In Bidirectional scan mode, one line is scanned in one direction, and the next line in the opposite direction. In other words, the return run, or flyback, of the laser beam is used for recording data as well, thus increasing the scanning frequency when using bidirectional scan mode. Refer to https://www.iso.org/standard/69820.html for further details and measurement methods.

children.name: Integration Number
children.description: Signal integration is generally utilized to improve the overall signal-to-noise ratio of the resulting Image. This field records the number of sequential Scanning Units (Line, Frame or Stack) that have been integrated for the Acquisition of this Channel.

children.name: Integration Unit
children.description: Signal integration is generally utilized to improve the overall signal-to-noise ratio of the resulting Image. This field refers to the nature of the Scanning Units used as the basis of signal integration. Refer to https://www.iso.org/standard/69820.html for further details and measurement methods.

children.name: Integration Method
children.description: Signal integration is generally utilized to improve the overall signal-to-noise ratio of the resulting Image. This field defines the signal Integration Method utilized for the Acquisition of this Channel.

children.name: Effective Zoom
children.description: This field describes the Effective Zoom factor that was utilized for the acquisition of this Image (e.g., zoom = 1.3). In confocal microscopy, it is possible to set a given hardware-based Zoom factor, aka "Confocal Zoom" or "Scan Zoom", by reducing the angle at which the scanning mirrors operate. As a result, the scanned area will be reduced in size, which (because the pixel areas associated with the scan are kept constant) will decrease the effective size of the portion of the sample that is projected on each pixel thus increasing the effective resolving power of the system. For example, with a 512x512 pixel area, and with a 40x objective, zoom 1 we result in a pixel size of 732nm, while zoom 10 will reduce the pixel size to 73nm).In confocal microscopy, it is possible to set a given hardware-based Zoom factor, aka "Confocal Zoom" or "Scan Zoom", by reducing the angle at which the scanning mirrors operate. As a result, the scanned area will be reduced in size, which (because the pixel areas associated with the scan are kept constant) will decrease the effective size of the portion of the sample that is projected on each pixel thus increasing the effective resolving power of the system. For example, with a 512x512 pixel area, and with a 40x objective, zoom 1 we result in a pixel size of 732nm, while zoom 10 will reduce the pixel size to 73nm).

children.name: Zoom Area Width
children.description: This field records the Width of the Zoom Area that was scanned during the Acquisition of this Channel, expressed in Pixels.

children.name: Zoom Area Height
children.description: This field records the Height of the Zoom Area that was scanned during the Acquisition of this Channel, expressed in Pixels.

children.name: Descanned
children.description: This field indicates whether or not the Acquisition of this Image involved descanning.

children.name: Scan Format
children.description: This field defines the format of the scanned element utilized for this Acquisition. Refer to https://www.iso.org/standard/69820.html for further details and measurement methods.

children.name: Multi Channel Mode
children.description: This field defines whether the the the Aquisiton of Multiple Channels for this Image happened in a Parallel (i.e., multiple channels acquired at the same time), or Sequentail (i.e., one channel after the other) manner.

children.name: Scan Time
children.description: This records the time in seconds required to acquire a given number of lines, frames or stacks including the time required for repositioning the scanning device to the starting point. Refer to https://www.iso.org/standard/69820.html for further details and measurement methods.

children.name: Confocal Field Number
children.description: This field recors the Field Number of the Confocal scan optic is expressed as the objective magnification times the maximum diameter of the scanned field; [mm], obtained without shifting the position of the specimen and without stitching single images into a total larger image. Because images generally have a square format, the diameter of the scanned field is equals to the square root of 2 times the length of X dimension of the scanned field. Refer to https://www.iso.org/standard/69820.html for further details and measurement methods.

children.name: Rotation
children.description: This field records the Rotation angle of the scan region.

children.name: Scanning Frequency
children.description: This field records the Scanning Frequency is defined at the ratio between the Number of scanning element (Lines, Frames or Stacks) acquired and ScanTime. Refer to https://www.iso.org/standard/69820.html for further details and measurement methods.

children.name: Diffraction Efficiency
children.description: This field records a measure of the performance efficiency of this device. It's a measure of how much optical power is diffracted into a designated direction compared to the power incident onto the diffractive element.

children.name: Acoustic Velocity
children.description: This field records the acoustic wave transit time across the crystal used during this Acquisition.

children.name: Acoustic Frequency
children.description: This field records the frequency of the acoustic wave used to alter the diffraction properties of the crystal during this Acquisition. Wavelength tuning is very rapid and limited only by the acoustic transit time across the crystal.

children.description: This is an empty element that refers to an Annotation (typically a Comment Annotation that consists of a simple multi-line comment) describing this component.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this element.

children.name: Binary File
children.description: Describes a binary file.

children.name: Size
children.description: Size of the uncompressed file. [unit:bytes]

children.name: External
children.description: Describes a file location. Can optionally specify a portion of a file using Offset and a ReadLength. If Offset and ReadLength are specified in conjuction with Compression, then they point into the uncompressed file.

children.name: href
children.description: file location

children.name: SHA1
children.description: The digest of the file specified in href.

children.name: Compression
children.description: Specifies the compression scheme used to encode the data.

children.name: Bin Data
children.description: The contents of this element are base64-encoded. These are not CDATA sections, just a base64 stream.

children.name: Compression
children.description: Specifies the compression scheme used to encode the data.

children.name: Big Endian
children.description: This is true if the binary data was written in BigEndian order. This is dependent on the system architecture of the machine that wrote the pixels. True for essentially all modern CPUs other than Intel and Alpha. All Binary data must be written in the same endian order.

children.name: Length
children.description: Character count attribute for the BinData field. This is the length of the base-64 encoded block. It allows easy skipping of the block when parsing the file. [unit:bytes]

children.name: Reflectance Profile File
children.description: This element represents a file in .csv or other suitable formats containing the Reflectance profile of this optical component.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this element.

children.name: Namespace
children.description: We recommend the inclusion of a namespace for annotations you define. If it is absent then we assume the annotation is to use our (OME's) default interpretation for this type.

children.name: Annotator
children.description: The Annotator is the person who attached this annotation.e.g. If UserA annotates something with TagB, owned by UserB, UserA is still the Annotator.

children.name: Transmission Wavelength Range Settings
children.description: This element records the range of Transmission Wavelengths that were set for this optical component during the Acquisition of this Image. It also records the maximum amount of light transmitted. Multiple of this elements can be used.

children.name: Cut On
children.description: CutOn is the Wavelength below which the response rate is below 50%. In case a CutOn Wavelength is not available, such as in the case of a ShortPass or Notch configuration, please enter 000.

children.name: Cut Off
children.description: CutOff is the Wavelength below which the response rate is below 50%. In case a CutOff Wavelength is not available, such as in the case of a LongPass configuration, please enter 000.

children.name: Transmittance
children.description: This field records the maximum amount of light this optical component is capable of transmitting in this Wavelength Range, at the peak Wavelength. It is represented as a fractional value from 0.0 to 1.0.

children.name: Reflection Wavelength Range Settings
children.description: This element records the range of Reflection Wavelengths that were set for this optical component during the Acquisition of this Image. It also records the maximum amount of light transmitted. Multiple of this elements can be used.

children.name: Cut On
children.description: CutOn is the Wavelength below which the response rate is below 50%. In case a CutOn Wavelength is not available, such as in the case of a ShortPass or Notch configuration, please enter 000.

children.name: Cut Off
children.description: CutOff is the Wavelength below which the response rate is below 50%. In case a CutOff Wavelength is not available, such as in the case of a LongPass configuration, please enter 000.

children.name: Reflectance
children.description: This field records the maximum amount of light this optical component is capable of reflecting in this Wavelength Range, at the peak Wavelength. It is represented as a fractional value from 0.0 to 1.0.

children.name: Acousto Optical Beam Splitter Settings
children.description: This element holds the Setting applied to an Acousto Optical Beam Splitter (AOBS) used during the Acquisition of this Channel as well as a reference (the ID attribute) to the AOBS in question.

children.name: ID
children.description: This is the Unique Identifier of the device used in this case.

children.name: Diffraction Efficiency
children.description: This field records a measure of the performance efficiency of this device. It's a measure of how much optical power is diffracted into a designated direction compared to the power incident onto the diffractive element.

children.name: Acoustic Velocity
children.description: This field records the acoustic wave transit time across the crystal used during this Acquisition.

children.name: Acoustic Frequency
children.description: This field records the frequency of the acoustic wave used to alter the diffraction properties of the crystal during this Acquisition. Wavelength tuning is very rapid and limited only by the acoustic transit time across the crystal.

children.name: Map Annotation
children.description: A Map Annotation. The contents of this is a list of Key/Value pairs.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this element.

children.name: Namespace
children.description: We recommend the inclusion of a namespace for annotations you define. If it is absent then we assume the annotation is to use our (OME's) default interpretation for this type.

children.name: Annotator
children.description: The Annotator is the person who attached this annotation.e.g. If UserA annotates something with TagB, owned by UserB, UserA is still the Annotator.

children.name: Description
children.description: This element contains a description for the annotation.[plain-text multi-line string]

children.name: Annotation Ref
children.description: This is an empty element that refers to an Annotation (typically a Comment Annotation that consists of a simple multi-line comment) describing this component.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this element.

children.name: Value
children.description: This is a Mapping of Key/Value pairs.

children.name: M
children.description: This is a Key/Value pair used to build up a Mapping. The Element and Attribute Name are kept to single letters to minimize the length at the expense of readability as they are likely to occur many times.

children.name: K
children.description: This is the key of this Key/Value pair.

children.name: Additional Vendor Information
children.description: This element is intendended to be used as a last-resort solution to capture vendor-specific Additional Information about this hardware component, which cannot be described using either available model elements (Classes or Attributes) or the Map Annotation feature of the schema. The use of this feature should not be intended as a way to hide information that the Imaging Community has designated as essential to ensure rigor and reproducibility. In case this feature is used, the Vendor is fully responsible for this information, including but not limited to, making sure this information is appropriately recorded and kept up to date.

children.name: Transmission Wavelength Range Settings
children.description: This element records the range of Transmission Wavelengths that were set for this optical component during the Acquisition of this Image. It also records the maximum amount of light transmitted. Multiple of this elements can be used.

children.name: Cut On
children.description: CutOn is the Wavelength below which the response rate is below 50%. In case a CutOn Wavelength is not available, such as in the case of a ShortPass or Notch configuration, please enter 000.

children.name: Cut Off
children.description: CutOff is the Wavelength below which the response rate is below 50%. In case a CutOff Wavelength is not available, such as in the case of a LongPass configuration, please enter 000.

children.name: Transmittance
children.description: This field records the maximum amount of light this optical component is capable of transmitting in this Wavelength Range, at the peak Wavelength. It is represented as a fractional value from 0.0 to 1.0.

children.name: Reflection Wavelength Range Settings
children.description: This element records the range of Reflection Wavelengths that were set for this optical component during the Acquisition of this Image. It also records the maximum amount of light transmitted. Multiple of this elements can be used.

children.description: CutOff is the Wavelength below which the response rate is below 50%. In case a CutOff Wavelength is not available, such as in the case of a LongPass configuration, please enter 000.

children.name: Reflectance
children.description: This field records the maximum amount of light this optical component is capable of reflecting in this Wavelength Range, at the peak Wavelength. It is represented as a fractional value from 0.0 to 1.0.

children.name: Electro Optical Device Settings Group
children.description: This element holds the Setting applied to an Electro-Optical Device used during the Acquisition of this Channel as well as a reference (the ID attribute) to the Electro-Optical Device in question.

children.name: Transmission Wavelength Range Settings

children.name: Cut On
children.description: CutOn is the Wavelength below which the response rate is below 50%. In case a CutOn Wavelength is not available, such as in the case of a ShortPass or Notch configuration, please enter 000.

children.name: Cut Off
children.description: CutOff is the Wavelength below which the response rate is below 50%. In case a CutOff Wavelength is not available, such as in the case of a LongPass configuration, please enter 000.

children.name: Transmittance
children.description: This field records the maximum amount of light this optical component is capable of transmitting in this Wavelength Range, at the peak Wavelength. It is represented as a fractional value from 0.0 to 1.0.

children.name: Reflection Wavelength Range Settings
children.description: This element records the range of Reflection Wavelengths that were set for this optical component during the Acquisition of this Image. It also records the maximum amount of light transmitted. Multiple of this elements can be used.

children.name: Cut On
children.description: CutOn is the Wavelength below which the response rate is below 50%. In case a CutOn Wavelength is not available, such as in the case of a ShortPass or Notch configuration, please enter 000.

children.description: CutOff is the Wavelength below which the response rate is below 50%. In case a CutOff Wavelength is not available, such as in the case of a LongPass configuration, please enter 000.

children.name: Reflectance

children.name: Liquid Crystal Tuneable Filter Settings
children.description: This element holds the Setting applied to a Liquid Crystal Tunable Filter (LCTF) used during the Acquisition of this Channel as well as a reference (the ID attribute) to the LCTF in question.

children.name: ID
children.description: This is the Unique Identifier of the device used in this case.

children.name: Polarization Rotation
children.description: A Pockels cell is a device consisting of an electro-optic crystal (with some electrodes attached to it) through which a light beam can propagate. The phase delay in the crystal (→ Pockels effect) can be modulated by applying a variable electric voltage. Alignment of the crystal axis with the light beam axis is critical. This field records the polarization rotation applied to the beam during the acquisition of this Channel.

children.name: TIRFSettings
children.description: Total Internal Reflection Fluorescence (TIRF) microscopy is a modality in which fluorescence is excited in a thin layer by an evanescent wave produced by total internal reflection. This element defines the TIRF conditions that were used for this Experiment.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this component.

children.name: HILO
children.description: This field capture whether (True) or not (False) TIRF illumination for this Experiment was set up at or below the critical angle (63˚; true TIRF) or with oblique illumination (i.e., Highly Inclined and Laminated Optical - HILO).

children.name: TIRFAngle
children.description: This field captures the incidence Angle used in this case.

children.name: TIRFHardware Module Ref
children.description: In case a specific TIRF Hardware Module was used for this TIRF experiment, this empty element refers to the TIRF Module that was used.

children.name: ID
children.description: A User-defined Name for this component.

children.name: TIRFLight Path
children.description: A description of the Ligth Path utilized for this TIRF experiment.

children.name: Description
children.description: This element holds a free text multi-line optional description for this component.

children.name: Light Path Map
children.description: This element holds a Map that records the order in which the different optical component associated with this Light Path are physically laid out.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this component.

children.name: Optical Component Position
children.description: This element records the Position of individual optical components in this Light Path.

children.name: ID
children.description: This is the ID of an existing Optical Component that is present in this Light Path.

children.name: Order
children.description: This field records the Positional order in which this optical component is found in this Light Path during the Acquisition of this Image.

children.name: Annotation Ref
children.description: This is an empty element that refers to an Annotation (typically a Comment Annotation that consists of a simple multi-line comment) describing this component.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this element.

children.name: Excitation Calibration
children.description: This element describes the procedure used to measure and calibrate the Wavelength and Irradiance of the Excitation Light Source used to Acquire this experimental Image and the resulting calibration measurements.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this component.

children.name: Date
children.description: Ths field records the Date in which this Calibration procedure was performed.

children.name: Sample Size
children.description: This field captures the Size of the Sample that was used to calculate the reported Intensity Calibration metrics. For example, this might represent the number of Illumination Power measurements that were utilized to calculate the Illumination Stability of a given Light Source.

children.name: Method
children.description: This field records a Description of Method used for this calibration procedure.

children.name: Spinning Disk In Light Path
children.description: In case of a Spinning Disk Confocal Microscope, this field records whether (True) or not (False)  the Spinning Disk was placed in the Light Path during this Calibration Procedure.

children.name: Nominal Ex-Wavelength
children.description: In case this is different from the Excitation Wavelength of the Channel this Calibration Procedure is associated with, this field records the Nominal Peak Excitation Wavelength  that is employed during this Procedure.

children.name: Observed Ex-Wavelength
children.description: This field records the observed Peak Excitation Wavelength at the Back Objective as measured by using this Calibration Procedure.

children.name: Percentage Nominal Power Settings
children.description: This field records the input % Nominal Illumination Power that was set during this Calibration Procedure as by the Manufacturer.

children.name: Observed Illumination Power At Back Of Objective
children.description: This field records the observed excitation Illumination Power (also known as Power Output or Illumination Intensity) as recorded  at the back focal plane of the Objective by using this Calibration Procedure. In order to collect this metric it is necessary to remove the Objective.

children.name: Observed Illumination Power At Back Objective Unit
Obsered Illumination Power At Sample
children.description: This field records the observed excitation Illumination Power (also known as Power Output or Illumination Intensity) as recorded  at the Sample (also referred to as the Front of the Objective) by using this Calibration Procedure. This metric is typically recorded with the Objective in place.

children.name: Obsered Illumination Power At Sample Unit
Short Term Illumination Stability
children.description: According to ISO 21073:2019, Illumination Stability is defined as the percentage deviation of Minimum and Maximum Illumination Power from the Average Power during the time interval considered. Specifically, SATB [%] = 100x {1-[(Pmax-Pmin)/Pmax+Pmin]}, where Pmax is the maximum laser power recorded during the time interval; and Pmin is the minimum laser power recorded during the time interval. This field records the Short Term Illumination Stability recorded during this Calibration Procedure by measuring Illumination Power every second for a time interval of 5 min of continuous illumination.

children.name: Long Term Illumination Stability
children.description: According to ISO 21073:2019, Illumination Stability is defined as the percentage deviation of Minimum and Maximum Illumination Power from the Average Power during the time interval considered. Specifically, SATB [%] = 100x {1-[(Pmax-Pmin)/Pmax+Pmin]}, where Pmax is the maximum laser power recorded during the time interval; and Pmin is the minimum laser power recorded during the time interval. This field records the Long Term Illumination Stability recorded during this Calibration Procedure by measuring Illumination Power every 30 s for one second for a time interval of 120 min of continuous illumination.

children.name: Illumination Linearity
children.description: This field captures the Linear Correlation Coefficient of observed Output Illumination Power response to different input Power settings, as measured at the back of the Objective.

children.name: Observed Irradiance At Sample
children.description: Observed mean excitation light irradiance (radiant power per unit area) as measured at the sample plane over the Field of View (FOV).

children.name: Observed Irradiance Variance
children.description: Observed excitation light irradiance (radiant power per unit area) variation over the Field of View (FOV).

children.name: Intensity Calibration Tool Ref
children.description: This element is a reference to the tool, if any, used to perform this calibratin procdure

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this component.

children.name: Intensity Calibration Report
children.description: This element refers to any File containing a report generated by the Calibration Software referred to in Calibration procedure.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this element.

children.name: Namespace
children.description: We recommend the inclusion of a namespace for annotations you define. If it is absent then we assume the annotation is to use our (OME's) default interpretation for this type.

children.name: Annotator
children.description: The Annotator is the person who attached this annotation.e.g. If UserA annotates something with TagB, owned by UserB, UserA is still the Annotator.

children.name: Description
children.description: This element contains a description for the annotation.[plain-text multi-line string]

children.description: This is an empty element that refers to an Annotation (typically a Comment Annotation that consists of a simple multi-line comment) describing this component.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this element.

children.name: Binary File
children.description: Describes a binary file.

children.name: Size
children.description: Size of the uncompressed file. [unit:bytes]

children.name: External
children.description: Describes a file location. Can optionally specify a portion of a file using Offset and a ReadLength. If Offset and ReadLength are specified in conjuction with Compression, then they point into the uncompressed file.

children.name: href
children.description: file location

children.name: SHA1
children.description: The digest of the file specified in href.

children.name: Compression
children.description: Specifies the compression scheme used to encode the data.

children.name: Bin Data
children.description: The contents of this element are base64-encoded. These are not CDATA sections, just a base64 stream.

children.name: Compression
children.description: Specifies the compression scheme used to encode the data.

children.name: Big Endian
children.description: This is true if the binary data was written in BigEndian order. This is dependent on the system architecture of the machine that wrote the pixels. True for essentially all modern CPUs other than Intel and Alpha. All Binary data must be written in the same endian order.

children.name: Length
children.description: Character count attribute for the BinData field. This is the length of the base-64 encoded block. It allows easy skipping of the block when parsing the file. [unit:bytes]

children.name: Calibration Software
children.description: This element describes a software tool utilized to analyze Calibration metrics obtained from this Calibration Procedure and generate a suitable Calibration Reports. As an example, such a Software tool might analyzes microscopy images of fluorescent Colored Beads in order to quantify the Resolution and Chromatic Registration of a fluorescence microsocope over the full Field of View (FOV). An example of such a tool is PSFj (http://www.knoplab.de/psfj/sample-page/). Alternatively, such a Software tool might be used to extract Intensity Calibration results obtained with a specific Intensity Calibration Tool, such as a Power Meter or MetaMax (https://www.umassmed.edu/grunwaldlab/).

children.name: Annotation Ref
children.description: This is a simple multi-line comment or annotation describing this component.

children.name: Annotation Ref
children.description: This is an empty element that refers to an Annotation (typically a Comment Annotation that consists of a simple multi-line comment) describing this component.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this element.

children.name: Additional Dimension Map
children.description: This map defines any additional dimension beyond the canonical 5 dimensions XYZCT. It contains a number of elements for each Plane of data referring to this Additional Dimension.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this component.

children.name: Name
children.description: A user assigned Name for this component.

children.name: Size
children.description: This field captures the number of sample points recorded along this dimension.

children.name: Type
children.description: This field describes the Type of this Additional Dimension.

children.name: Type Description
children.description: This is a description of the Type used to indicate to an application or user how the data should be interpreted.

children.name: Order
children.description: This field describes the order in which the individual Planes of data referring to this dimension are interleaved in the Image past the canonical 5 dimension XYZCT. The number should be zero based, e.g., 0, 1, 2 etc.

children.name: Preset Step Increment
children.description: This field records the preset size of Step Increment between Planes of data along this dimension. The Increment is numbered from 0, with default value of 1. Setting a value for this attribute to -1 indicates non-uniform sampling along this Additional Dimension. In this case the actual Step Increment value associated with each Plane along this Additional Dimension will have to be stored in TheIncrementStamp attribute of TheAdditionalDimension.

children.name: Unit
children.description: This field records the Unit used for the metric recorded along this dimension

children.name: The Additional Dimension
children.description: This element refers to each individual Plane of data pertaining to a given Additional Dimension.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this component.

children.name: The Additional Dimension Index
children.description: This field records the Index of this specific Plane within a vector of Additional Dimension data.

children.name: The Increment Stamp
children.description: This field records the actual Step Increment value associated with each Plane of data along this Additional Dimension vector for this Image. While, this field is only required with non-uniform sampling along this Additional Dimension (as indicated by a value of -1 stored in AdditionalDImension/@ActualStepIncrement), it is always allowed.

children.name: Description
children.description: This element holds a free text multi-line optional description for this component.

children.description: This is an empty element that refers to an Annotation (typically a Comment Annotation that consists of a simple multi-line comment) describing this component.

children.name: ID
children.description: A Unique Identifier for this element.

children.name: Bin Data
children.description: The contents of this element are base64-encoded. These are not CDATA sections, just a base64 stream.

children.name: Compression

children.name: Big Endian
children.description: This is true if the binary data was written in BigEndian order. This is dependent on the system architecture of the machine that wrote the pixels. True for essentially all modern CPUs other than Intel and Alpha. All Binary data must be written in the same endian order.

children.name: Length
children.description: Character count attribute for the BinData field. This is the length of the base-64 encoded block. It allows easy skipping of the block when parsing the file. [unit:bytes]

children.name: Metadata Only
children.description: This place holder means there is no pixel data in this file.

children.name: NGFFData
children.description: This element describes the location of the pixel data in a NGFF storage set.

children.name: Tiff Data
children.description: This field describes the location of the pixel data in a tiff file.

children.name: IFD
children.description: Gives the IFD(s) for which this element is applicable. Indexed from 0. Default is 0 (the first IFD). [units:none]

children.name: First Z
children.description: Gives the Z position of the Image Plane at the specified IFD. Indexed from 0. Default is 0 (the first Z position). [units:none]

children.name: First T
children.description: Gives the T position of the Image Plane at the specified IFD. Indexed from 0. Default is 0 (the first T position). [units:none]

children.name: First C
children.description: Gives the C position of the Image Plane at the specified IFD. Indexed from 0. Default is 0 (the first C position). [units:none]

children.name: Plane Count
children.description: Gives the number of IFDs affected. Dimension order of IFDs is given by the enclosing Pixels element's DimensionOrder attribute. Default is the number of IFDs in the TIFF file, unless an IFD is specified, in which case the default is 1. [units:none]

children.name: UUID
children.description: This must be used when the IFDs are located in another file. Note: It is permissible for this to be self referential.

children.name: File Name
children.description: This can be used when the IFDs are located in another file. The / (forward slash) is used as the path separator. A relative path is recommended. However an absolute path can be specified. Default is to use the file the ome-xml data has been pulled from. Note: It is permissible for this to be self referential. The file image1.tiff may contain ome-xml data that has FilePath="image1.tiff" or "./image1.tiff"

children.name: K

children.name: ROI
children.description: A four dimensional 'Region of Interest'. If they are not used, and the Image has more than one Plane, the entire set of Planes is assumed to be included in the ROI. Multiple ROIs may be specified.

children.name: Name
children.description: The Name identifies the ROI to the user. [plain-text string]

children.name: Rectangle
children.description: A simple rectangle object. If rotation is required apply a transformation at the Shape level.

children.name: Fill Color
children.description: The color of the fill - encoded as RGBA The value "-1" is #FFFFFFFF so solid white (it is a signed 32 bit value) NOTE: Prior to the 2012-06 schema the default value was incorrect and produced a transparent red not solid white.

children.name: Stroke Color
children.description: The color of the stroke - encoded as RGBA The value "-1" is #FFFFFFFF so solid white (it is a signed 32 bit value) NOTE: Prior to the 2012-06 schema the default value was incorrect and produced a transparent red not solid white.

children.name: Stroke Width
children.description: The width of the stroke. Units are set by StrokeWidthUnit.

children.name: Stroke Width Unit
children.description: The units used for the stroke width.

children.name: Font Size
children.description: Size of the font. Units are set by FontSizeUnit.

children.name: Font Size Unit
children.description: The units used for the font size.

children.name: Locked
children.description: Controls whether the shape is locked and read only, true is locked, false is editable.

children.name: The Z
children.description: The z-section the ROI applies to. If not specified then the ROI applies to all the z-sections of the Image. [units:none] This is numbered from 0.

children.name: The T
children.description: The timepoint the ROI applies to. If not specified then the ROI applies to all the timepoints of the Image. [units:none] This is numbered from 0.

children.name: The C
children.description: The Channel the ROI applies to. If not specified then the ROI applies to all the channels of the Image. [units:none] This is numbered from 0.

children.name: X
children.description: The X value of the left edge or the rectangle. [units pixels]

children.name: Y
children.description: The y value of the top edge or the rectangle. [units pixels]

children.name: Width
children.description: The width of the rectangle. [units pixels]

children.name: Height
children.description: The height of the rectangle. [units pixels]

children.name: Transform
children.description: This is a matrix used to transform the shape. The element has 6 xsd:float attributes. If the element is present then all 6 values must be included.

children.name: Stroke Width Unit
children.description: The units used for the stroke width.

children.name: Font Size
children.description: Size of the font. Units are set by FontSizeUnit.

children.name: Font Size Unit
children.description: The units used for the font size.

children.name: Locked
children.description: Controls whether the shape is locked and read only, true is locked, false is editable.

children.name: The Z
children.description: The z-section the ROI applies to. If not specified then the ROI applies to all the z-sections of the Image. [units:none] This is numbered from 0.

children.name: The T
children.description: The timepoint the ROI applies to. If not specified then the ROI applies to all the timepoints of the Image. [units:none] This is numbered from 0.

children.name: The C
children.description: The Channel the ROI applies to. If not specified then the ROI applies to all the channels of the Image. [units:none] This is numbered from 0.

children.name: X
children.description: The X coordinate of the point. [units pixels]

children.name: Y
children.description: The Y coordinate of the point. [units pixels]

children.name: Transform
children.description: This is a matrix used to transform the shape. The element has 6 xsd:float attributes. If the element is present then all 6 values must be included.

children.name: Ellipse
children.description: A simple ellipse object. If rotation is required apply a transformation at the Shape level.

children.name: Binary Only
children.description: Pointer to an external metadata file. If this element is present, then no other metadata may be present in this file, i.e. this file is a place-holder.

children.name: Metadata File
children.description: Filename of the OME-XML metadata file for this binary data. If the file cannot be found, a search can be performed based on the UUID.

children.name: UUID
children.description: The unique identifier of another OME-XML block whose metadata describes the binary data in this file. This UUID is considered authoritative regardless of mismatches in the filename.
